agencyVerdicts.data_explanation: The 3,392 flagged rows (475 triple_gap are a strict subset of the 3,392 placeholder_payee) are an internal-accounting convention, not missing-accountability disbursements. Evidence from mart/tier_detail.parquet (agency='DEPT OF ECONOMIC SECURITY', flagged by triple_gap OR placeholder_payee): (1) Payee is the literal placeholder 'N/A' (2,824 rows, $1.54B) or NULL (565 rows, $250M) on EVERY row except 3 — the placeholder_payee marker is just keying off the 'N/A'/NULL string. (2) Payment method is internal book-entry on 99.96% of dollars: NULL ($864M), INTERNAL ($524M), COST ALLOCATION ($269M), JOURNAL VOUCHER ($134M). Only 3 transactions ($637,973 = 0.04% of dollars) are real external rails (1 WARRANT to 'MISCELLANEOUS VENDOR' $325K, 2 ACH to 'PCARD VENDOR DEA' $313K), and even those are themselves masked/placeholder vendors. ZERO flagged rows have a named external payee. (3) Appropriations are explicitly clearing/cost-allocation/non-appropriated buckets: '...CLEARING', 'OCCP CLR' (occupancy clearing), 'OCCUPANCY CLR', 'ADAMS BLDG CLR', 'INSTITUTIONAL CLR', 'OPER NON-APPR', 'POST/SUPP CLR' — i.e. inter-fund moves and overhead allocations across DES divisions (DDD, DCSS, DERS, DAAS, LTC). (4) transaction_description is N/A or NULL on all but 98 rows; the lone non-null text is 'ONLY UPDATE THE DESCRIPTION FOR FY18 AND GOING FORWARD' — an internal bookkeeping note, not a purpose. (5) The single largest appropriation, DDD PREMIUM TAX LTC ($285M flagged; $285M total in hv_base), is 100% INTERNAL/JOURNAL VOUCHER premium-tax accruals on the Long-Term-Care Medicaid managed-care program — benign-by-structure per the known caveats. (6) Systemic, not targeted: agency-wide, placeholder ('N/A'/NULL) payees cover 92,306 of ~220,000 DES high-value rows (42% of rows, $24.6B / 52% of dollars), so a placeholder payee is the house style for DES book entries, not an anomaly. Per discipline (read, never infer), the data itself — internal rails + clearing appropriations + null descriptions/payees — explains these as routine inter-fund/cost-allocation accounting texture. The right action is to fix/annotate the placeholder_payee and triple_gap markers (they fire on internal book entries that have no payee by design) rather than treat these as review-worthy outflows.
agencyVerdicts.note: Recommend marker fixes for overtaker.ai: (a) gate placeholder_payee/triple_gap to EXTERNAL rails only — exclude payment_method IN (NULL,'INTERNAL','COST ALLOCATION','JOURNAL VOUCHER'), which would clear ~99.96% of this cluster's dollars; (b) treat appropriation names containing 'CLR'/'CLEARING'/'NON-APPR'/'COST ALLOC' as internal-fund context that suppresses the discretionary/null-payee gap. Residual genuine review surface here is negligible: only $0.64M across 3 masked-vendor external payments (MISCELLANEOUS VENDOR warrant, PCARD VENDOR DEA ACH) is even arguably reviewable, and those would be better caught by the masked_payee marker than by placeholder_payee. Note the known FY2019/2020 NULL vendor_id caveat is consistent with the FY-flat then FY2021+ rise in counts; it does not change the verdict. Net: explained_benign / data-transparency texture, low bondholder interest.

agencyVerdicts.data_explanation: All 2,299 flagged DOT items (the 1,523 triple_gap are a subset, every flagged row carries placeholder_payee) are internal-accounting ledger postings, not vendor disbursements. Evidence read directly from the data: (1) Rails: 1,873 of 2,299 are JOURNAL VOUCHER (1,029) or INTERNAL (844); the remaining are NULL pm (380) or an ACH P-card aggregator 'PCARD VENDOR DTA' (46). No WARRANT/standard vendor-payment rail. (2) Payee/description: payee is exclusively 'N/A' (1,449), NULL (804) or the P-card aggregator; transaction_description is 'N/A' or NULL on essentially every row -- because these are internal postings with no invoice, not redacted vendor payments. (3) Appropriations are core DOT programs: FEDERAL AID, HIGHWAY CONSTRUCTION, M-RARF CONSTRUCT FREEWAYS, I-17 EXPANSION, SR189 CONSTRUCTION, STATE FLEET OPERATIONS, DEBT SERVICE, OPERATING LUMP SUM, STANDARD ALLOCATION (cost-allocation), 2.6 PERCENT / 24PERCENT DISTRIBUTION (statutory fund splits) -- all inter-fund/capitalization/allocation mechanics. (4) Corroboration in hv_base: the same capital appropriations (M-RARF freeways, I-17 Expansion) show large NAMED-vendor spend on ACH/WARRANT rails (e.g. M-RARF ~$540M ACH named, I-17 ~$167M ACH named), while only the JV/INTERNAL/NULL counterpart rows are blank-payee -- i.e. the real contractor payments are visible and named; these flagged rows are their internal-ledger twin. (5) Systemic, not DOT-specific: placeholder_payee fires across 70 agencies; dataset-wide COST ALLOCATION carries a named payee in 0 of 51,374 rows, INTERNAL in 11 of 63,590 -- a structural property of these rails. P-card line (PCARDDTA0001) is a documented payee-redaction convention for small fleet/vehicle purchases. Net: triple_gap/placeholder_payee here = a data-transparency texture of the internal-accounting layer, not missing-accountability spending.
agencyVerdicts.note: DOWNWEIGHT/annotate as a systemic accounting convention, do not surface as per-item leads. The blank payee on JOURNAL VOUCHER / INTERNAL / COST ALLOCATION / NULL rails is structural (a 'payee' is inapplicable to inter-fund and cost-allocation postings), confirmed dataset-wide (named-payee rate ~0% on COST ALLOCATION and INTERNAL). The marker is essentially re-flagging the internal-accounting rail. Two narrow, optional follow-ups if a bondholder wants reassurance, none rising to genuine_review on this evidence: (a) reconcile the JV/INTERNAL totals per capital appropriation (M-RARF, I-17, SR189) against the named ACH/WARRANT contractor spend on the same appropriation to confirm the internal entries net to real, named outlays; (b) spot-check the few JOURNAL VOUCHER rows that DO carry a named payee dataset-wide (4,818 of 16,197) since JV is the one internal rail occasionally used for actual disbursements. Marker-fix recommendation: scope placeholder_payee/triple_gap to EXCLUDE rows whose payment_method is INTERNAL / COST ALLOCATION / JOURNAL VOUCHER / NULL (the internal-accounting rails) so it stops re-firing on the ledger layer; the manual_rail_disc component is likewise definitional here.

agencyVerdicts.data_explanation: The DEPT OF ADMINISTRATION is Arizona's central internal-service-fund / statewide-accounting agency, and the triple_gap and placeholder_payee markers fire on the structural texture of that role rather than on missing accountability. Of the ~$975M flagged, 99.94% by dollars (813 of 818 items, $974.8M) are NON-EXTERNAL book entries: payment_method is INTERNAL (529 items / $778M), COST ALLOCATION ($40M), JOURNAL VOUCHER ($16M), or NULL ($142M) — i.e. inter-fund transfers and cost allocations that BY DESIGN have no external payee, so payee shows as 'N/A' or NULL. The largest flagged dollars are benign-by-structure: (1) COP DEBT SERVICE 2009 (Certificates of Participation capital-financing debt service), ~$430M, INTERNAL rail to the trustee/debt-service account; and (2) NON APPROPRIATED BENEFITS PROGRAM run through the SPECIAL EMPLOYEE HEALTH INSURANCE TRUST FUND / EMPLOYEE BENEFIT PLAN FUND — the State's self-insured employee health/benefits trust. Cross-checking hv_base, that same appropriation overwhelmingly pays NAMED real insurers (Blue Cross Blue Shield AZ, MedImpact, UnitedHealthcare, Aetna, Cigna/Connecticut General, Delta Dental — multiple billions); the flagged 'N/A' rows are the INTERNAL trust-fund accounting legs of that program, not hidden recipients. The remainder is textbook internal-service-fund activity: Risk Management losses/premiums, Workers Comp, Construction Insurance, Building Renewal, Inter-Agency Service Agreements (ISAs), and HRIS/E-Procurement IT projects — all single-payee inter-fund moves. Only 5 items ($544K total, 0.06%) sit on an actual external rail (ACH/PAYMENT) with a placeholder; these carry masked_payee markers and are Workers Comp / employee bus payments, i.e. privacy-redacted individual claimants/employees, not concealed vendors. Net: the null-payee + manual-rail pattern is a routine central-accounting / data-transparency convention, not a per-item accountability gap.
agencyVerdicts.note: DOWNWEIGHT/annotate the cluster. The triple_gap and placeholder_payee markers are firing structurally on an internal-service-fund agency: 813/818 flagged items ($974.8M, 99.94%) are non-external book entries (INTERNAL/COST ALLOCATION/JOURNAL VOUCHER/NULL rail) — inter-fund moves, debt service, and benefits-trust accounting legs that legitimately have no external payee. Top dollars are COP debt service (~$430M, benign-by-structure) and the self-insured employee health/benefits trust (which pays named insurers in hv_base; the N/A rows are its trust-fund legs). MARKER FIX SUGGESTION: suppress/downweight triple_gap and placeholder_payee when payment_method is INTERNAL, COST ALLOCATION, JOURNAL VOUCHER, or NULL (inter-fund book entries), and when the appropriation is COP DEBT SERVICE* or NON APPROPRIATED BENEFITS PROGRAM. The only residual review-worthy slice is the 5 external-rail (ACH/PAYMENT) items totaling $544K, which carry masked_payee and appear to be privacy-redacted individual claimants (Workers Comp / employee bus payments) — minor and explained. No genuine concealed-vendor accountability gap found in the sampled cluster.

agencyVerdicts.data_explanation: All 631 flagged items ($746.8M) are aggregate retailer-commission accruals booked to an internal control account, not discretionary vendor payments. Signature is uniform: 100% NULL/N-A payee (0 named payees across the entire set AND across all 9,731 Lottery HV rows on the commission appropriation), 100% INTERNAL payment method (the agency's dominant rail, 3,798 of its HV rows), 100% N-A/NULL description. 92% of dollars sit on appropriation 'SALES COMMISSIONS TO TICKET RETAILERS' / 'COMMISSIONS TO CHARITABLE ORGANIZATIONS'; 99.7% are category OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES. Cadence is a stable ~$60-100M/year recurring accrual every FY 2016-2025 (~$1M avg line) -- the statutory ~6.5% retailer commission, the single largest line in any lottery, booked in aggregate rather than per-retailer. The markers misfire structurally: placeholder_payee fires on the agency's universal redacted/aggregate-payee convention; manual_rail_disc fires on its standard INTERNAL rail; triple_gap compounds these with vendor_share_of_agency_year, which is NULL for the whole set because there is no vendor_id to compute a share. Structural caveats confirm benignity: the 160 NULL-appropriation flagged items ($151M) fall ENTIRELY in FY2019-2020, the known degraded-metadata years, and their category/amount/cadence match the commission line; the Tier-1 peer_outlier rows (avg z=25) are merely the largest aggregate accrual lines. No genuine discretionary, concentrated, or individual-vendor item is present.
agencyVerdicts.note: Recommend downweight/annotate, not per-item review. One marker-quality fix worth surfacing to the modeling team: triple_gap/placeholder_payee fire system-wide on the Lottery's aggregate-commission control account, where a null payee, INTERNAL rail, and uncomputable vendor share are by-design (no per-retailer vendor identity exists). The FY2019-2020 NULL-appropriation subset is the documented metadata-degradation artifact, not a hidden program. A legitimately review-worthy version of this lead would be the underlying per-retailer commission detail (which the checkbook does not expose) -- a transparency gap, not an accountability finding. File paths: /Users/moeasnani/Documents/Openbooks/mart/tier_detail.parquet, /Users/moeasnani/Documents/Openbooks/mart/hv_base.parquet.

agencyVerdicts.data_explanation: All 653 flagged items (~$672.3M) carry vid=NULL, payee in {N/A, NULL, MISCELLANEOUS VENDOR}, and transaction_description=N/A/NULL — the markers fire on blank fields, not on anomalous payments. This blank-payee state is the dominant texture of the entire agency: in hv_base.parquet, 53,576 of 69,982 DOC high-value rows (76.6%, ~$5.27B) have a placeholder/null payee, versus AHCCCS 8.3%, Dept of Education 8.3%, DOT 30.1%; when DOC does populate payee the vendors are real and named (3M, Ameresco, ALR Construction, inmate-trust accounts). So placeholder_payee and the null-vendor leg of triple_gap are over-firing on an agency-wide publication convention. Caveat 1 applies (all 653 have NULL vid). manual_rail_disc/triple_gap ride on payment_method=INTERNAL (inter-fund / cost-allocation rail), a legitimate internal accounting convention rather than discretionary cash. The appropriation names identify the spend as routine recurring programs: Private Prison Per Diem / Private Prison COP (private-prison contracts on the internal rail), Inmate Health Care Contracted Services + Hepatitis C Treatment RX + Non-Contract Medication (correctional-healthcare contract and pharma spend), and recurring NON-HEALTH CARE AOOE inter-fund allocations — all recurring year over year FY2016-2025. Only 3 items (~$1.94M, MISCELLANEOUS VENDOR + masked_payee, WARRANT rail) are a small deliberate redacted-payee set, still a convention. Files: /Users/moeasnani/Documents/Openbooks/mart/tier_detail.parquet, /Users/moeasnani/Documents/Openbooks/mart/hv_base.parquet.
agencyVerdicts.note: Data-transparency/publication-convention artifact, not missing-accountability. Recommended action: fix the markers, not escalate the transactions — suppress placeholder_payee/triple_gap where an entire agency runs on placeholder payees (DOC at 76.6%), and drop the null-vid leg given the system-wide NULL vendor_id caveat. Residual low-priority follow-up only if a bondholder wants the named counterparties behind Private Prison Per Diem and the Inmate Health Care contract, which are identifiable from the appropriation lines.

agencyVerdicts.data_explanation: The 1,337 flagged DCS items are an internal-accounting convention, not missing-accountability items. Evidence from mart/tier_detail.parquet and mart/hv_base.parquet: (1) PAYEE — 1,336 of 1,337 carry payee 'N/A'/NULL with vendor_id_code '00000000000000000000' or NULL; the only real payee is 'MISCELLANEOUS VENDOR' (itself a placeholder rollup, a $101K warrant). (2) RAIL — only 1 of 1,337 is a WARRANT (real cash disbursement); the remaining 1,336 ride NULL / COST ALLOCATION / INTERNAL / JOURNAL VOUCHER — non-cash internal rails. The 'triple_gap' subset (253) is 100% JOURNAL VOUCHER + INTERNAL by construction. (3) STRUCTURAL PROOF the placeholder_payee marker is deterministic, not diagnostic: across the entire state checkbook (hv_base), INTERNAL, COST ALLOCATION, and PAYMENT rails are 100% null-payee, while ACH and WARRANT are 0% null-payee. So placeholder_payee fires whenever a row sits on an internal rail — it is a function of payment_method, not a per-item accountability gap. (4) APPROPRIATIONS — DCS operating/caseworker 'clearing' accounts, operating lump sums, adoption/AG-legal services, and Health Plan (CMDP/AHCCCS) Premium Tax / Services / Administration expenditure authorities: inter-fund allocations and Medicaid managed-care for foster children, benign-by-structure. (5) Recurring identical amounts (6,826,600 in FY24 & FY25; 2,998,700; 4,736,233) are the signature of scheduled inter-fund / cost-allocation moves, not discretionary vendor payments. (6) DESCRIPTIONS are 100% 'N/A'/NULL or a generic ledger-maintenance boilerplate ('ONLY UPDATE THE DESCRIPTION FOR FY18...') — there is no per-transaction discretionary narrative to review. This is a data-transparency texture (redacted/placeholder payee on internal rails), not discretionary outbound spend to unaccountable parties.
agencyVerdicts.note: DOWNWEIGHT/annotate as a marker artifact. The placeholder_payee marker should be suppressed (or treated as a structural label) whenever payment_method is in {INTERNAL, COST ALLOCATION, PAYMENT, JOURNAL VOUCHER}, since those rails are 100%/70% null-payee state-wide by design; firing it on every internal-rail row generated all 1,337 DCS leads with near-zero discrimination. Likewise triple_gap = null-vendor + manual-rail + discretionary collapses to 'this is a journal voucher / internal move' for DCS and adds little. Keep one residual, auditor-level (not transaction-level) caveat per the team's READ-don't-infer discipline: this verdict certifies the MARKERS misfired on internal rails; it does not certify the underlying cost-allocation/inter-fund methodology or the FY2020 CAPITAL EQUIPMENT reclass spike (~$85M posted on NULL appropriation/NULL rail), which an auditor reviews at the methodology/journal-entry level rather than per item. Relevant files: /Users/moeasnani/Documents/Openbooks/mart/tier_detail.parquet and /Users/moeasnani/Documents/Openbooks/mart/hv_base.parquet.

agencyVerdicts.data_explanation: All 718 flagged items (triple_gap + placeholder_payee, ~$511M) share one structural fingerprint: they are INTERNAL payment-method rows with payee and transaction_description both null/N/A, and null vid, contract_name, and contract_number. The 'triple_gap' (null-vendor + manual-rail + discretionary) and 'placeholder_payee' markers are therefore re-detecting the same single fact: this is an INTERNAL-rail (manual/journal) transaction. That null-payee-on-INTERNAL is a STATEWIDE accounting convention, not a DHS gap: across mart/hv_base.parquet, 63,579 of 63,590 INTERNAL high-value rows (99.98%) have a null/N/A payee, and every major agency (AHCCCS, Corrections, DPS, DES, ADOT, Education) uses the same rail at higher volume than DHS. The appropriations behind these rows are legitimate program funds: PUBLIC HEALTH EMERGENCIES ($154M), FEDERAL GRANTS ($125M), ARIZONA STATE HOSPITAL OPERATING ($53M), AGENCYWIDE OPERATING LUMP SUM, ISA/IGA agreements, and CORONAVIRUS DISEASE 2019. The dominant FY2021 spike (~$221M, vs $18-35M in normal years) is 88% Public Health Emergencies/COVID/Federal Grant appropriations -- i.e. pandemic federal-money inter-fund allocations and pass-throughs, exactly what the manual rail carries. These are inter-fund moves / cost allocations / federally-funded program disbursements booked via journal entries, where no external vendor name is captured by design -- a data-transparency texture, not missing-accountability spending to a hidden party. No recoverable counterparty exists in ANY field, so per-item review is not actionable. Caveats 1 (NULL vendor_id FY2019-20) and 2 (contract_number ~4% populated) directly explain the null-vendor and off-contract components of triple_gap, confirming these are weak structural markers here.
agencyVerdicts.note: Recommend DOWNWEIGHT/annotate, not per-item KEEP. The triple_gap and placeholder_payee markers should be collapsed: on the INTERNAL rail, null payee is the systemic convention (99.98% statewide), so both markers are essentially proxies for payment_method='INTERNAL' and should not independently inflate risk_score (current scores 12.6-13.5 are driven by the redundant stack peer_outlier+triple_gap+placeholder_payee). The one residual analytic value for a bondholder is aggregate transparency texture, not individual transactions: DHS routes ~$511M of high-value activity through manual journal entries that capture no external counterparty -- worth a single agency-level note about disclosure completeness on COVID/federal-grant funds, but NOT 718 review-worthy leads. Suggested marker fixes: (a) suppress placeholder_payee when payment_method='INTERNAL'; (b) require triple_gap to have at least one non-structural signal before scoring; (c) treat the FY2021 Public Health Emergencies concentration as COVID-explained.

agencyVerdicts.data_explanation: All 681 flagged ASRS items (532 triple_gap + 149 placeholder_payee-only) share one identical structural signature: payment_method='INTERNAL', payee is literally 'N/A'/NULL, transaction_description is 'N/A'/NULL, and contract_number is NULL. There is no per-item variation to review. By dollars, the cluster is overwhelmingly the ASRS retiree benefit programs: HEALTH INSURANCE SUBSIDY ($184.9M, 434 rows) and HEALTH INSURANCE ($117.9M, 244 rows) on the INTERNAL rail, plus a small tail of OPERATING LUMP SUM APPROPRIATION / ADMINISTRATIVE EXPENSES operating cost-allocation entries. ASRS is benign-by-structure (pension system) per the known caveats. Critically, the named-payee twin of this exact appropriation in hv_base.parquet pays UNITED HEALTHCARE SERVICES INC via ACH/WARRANT — i.e. the flagged INTERNAL/null-payee rows are the SAME retiree health-insurance premium subsidy program, disbursed through an internal accounting rail that records the payee as 'N/A'/NULL rather than naming the carrier/retirees. The three markers fired purely on structure: placeholder_payee (payee=='N/A'), triple_gap (null-vendor + INTERNAL/manual rail + discretionary category), and manual_rail_disc (INTERNAL==manual rail). vendor_share_of_agency_year is NULL across every row (share is uncomputable with no vendor_id), so the 'discretionary/concentrated' leg of triple_gap never reflected a real concentrated vendor — it reflects only the missing payee field. The high robust_z (up to ~15) is just the size of statutory benefit disbursements, not an anomalous vendor.
agencyVerdicts.note: Routine internal-accounting / redacted-payee convention, not missing accountability. This is a single homogeneous block (one rail, one redaction pattern) covering a statutory ASRS retiree health-insurance benefit whose named-payee equivalent resolves to United Healthcare Services Inc, plus minor operating cost-allocation entries. Recommendation: DOWNWEIGHT the whole cluster and FIX the markers — placeholder_payee/triple_gap/manual_rail_disc are all keying off payment_method='INTERNAL' + null payee, which is systematic data texture on Arizona's checkbook for internal-rail benefit disbursements, not a transaction-level red flag. To make triple_gap meaningful, require a real concentrated/anomalous signal (non-null vendor_share or a computable concentration) rather than firing on the mere absence of a payee. Low overtaker/bondholder interest: no discretionary, concentrated, or unusual vendor exposure here.

agencyVerdicts.data_explanation: The null-payee / "placeholder_payee" condition is a deterministic property of the payment RAIL, not a per-item redaction or accountability gap. All 776 flagged AHCCCS items are payment_method=INTERNAL (1 is JOURNAL VOUCHER), and 100.0% of INTERNAL rows at AHCCCS (11,134/11,134) have null/N-A payee. Checkbook-wide the same holds: INTERNAL, COST ALLOCATION, and PAYMENT rails are 100.0% null-payee by construction (vs 0.0% for ACH/WARRANT). The "triple_gap" marker (null-vendor + manual-rail + discretionary) re-detects the SAME single structural fact three ways, so it is not three independent red flags. transaction_description is uninformative (775/777 are "N/A"/NULL), so the markers had nothing payee-level to latch onto and fired on the rail. The appropriation names explain the money as inter-agency / inter-fund moves: ADOA DATA CENTER (AZ Dept of Administration IT chargeback, ~$71.7M), ARPA federal initiatives passed through to DES/ADHS (~$92.5M), DES ELIGIBILITY (DES performs Medicaid eligibility determination for AHCCCS), PROP 204 (Medicaid expansion admin), and OPERATING LUMP SUM allocations. These are precisely the IGA / cost-allocation / pass-through class that is benign-by-structure. The flagged items are spread evenly across every fiscal year 2016-2025, the signature of a standing accounting convention rather than a discrete event.
agencyVerdicts.note: Verdict: explained_benign, with a marker-fix recommendation. The placeholder_payee and triple_gap markers should be suppressed (or heavily downweighted) for payment_method in (INTERNAL, COST ALLOCATION, PAYMENT, JOURNAL VOUCHER), where null payee is 100% structural — these rails are inter-fund/cost-allocation moves that never carry an external vendor by design. As written the marker generates ~777 false-signal items at AHCCCS alone for a single underlying fact. Residual to annotate (NOT dismiss): the FY2024 spike ($115M, vs ~$20-40M other years) driven by repeated identical $16.2M "EPD ARPA INITIATIVES" INTERNAL chargebacks, and the ADOA Data Center concentration. These are appropriation-named federal-ARPA pass-throughs and IT chargebacks, not hidden vendors, but their size/repetition merits a light annotation for a bondholder rather than full inclusion. Net: data-transparency texture (the AZ checkbook simply does not name a payee on internal rails), not a missing-accountability finding. Queried tier_detail.parquet and hv_base.parquet only.

agencyVerdicts.data_explanation: The flagged DPS items resolve to a single, agency-wide accounting/publishing convention rather than missing-accountability events. Of the ~750 flagged rows (~$192.5M), payee is 'N/A' or NULL on ~630 rows ($183.7M) and payment_method is 'INTERNAL' on 627 rows ($183.7M); the remainder are aggregated-card placeholders 'PCARD VENDOR PSA' (vid PCARDPSA0001, 55 rows, $7.3M, ACH) and 'FUEL VENDOR PSA' (vid FUELPSA00001, 5 rows, $0.6M) — synthetic roll-up vendors for p-card and fuel-card spend, not real persons. transaction_description is 'N/A' or NULL on 100% of the flagged set (zero distinct non-null descriptions), so there is no per-item narrative to read by design. The only populated descriptive field, appropriation_1_name, maps entirely to ordinary law-enforcement operations: DPS JOINT FUND, MOTOR VEHICLE FUEL, CAPITAL EQUIPMENT (vehicle replacement, lab/scientific analysis, communications), HIGHWAY PATROL, CRIMINAL INVESTIGATIONS, and border task forces. category1 is 96%+ OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES + CAPITAL EQUIPMENT ($178M of $192.5M). The pattern is pervasive, not isolated: across hv_base.parquet the entire agency carries ~$1.9B of NULL-rail N/A-payee, $1.18B PAYMENT-rail N/A-payee, and $658M INTERNAL-rail N/A-payee rows — i.e. the null-payee/internal-rail texture is the State of Arizona checkbook's standard treatment for internal-fund moves, cost allocations, and card-aggregated spend, not a DPS-specific gap. It recurs every fiscal year 2016-2025 at steady volume. triple_gap (null-vendor + manual-rail + discretionary) and placeholder_payee are therefore firing on the data-transparency convention itself; the agency_benford / period13 / june_round co-markers add nothing diagnostic on top of that. These are false-positive-style structural misfires of the markers (publishing convention), and as a class they downweight to routine.
agencyVerdicts.note: Verdict: explained_benign / systemic convention. The 510 triple_gap + 690 placeholder_payee items are a single Arizona-checkbook publishing artifact: 100% have N/A/NULL transaction_description, ~84% are N/A/NULL payee on the INTERNAL rail, and the small ACH remainder is synthetic card-aggregation vendors (PCARD VENDOR PSA / FUEL VENDOR PSA). All populated appropriations are ordinary DPS operations (fuel, fleet, equipment, labs, patrol, investigations, border task forces). The same null-payee/internal-rail texture spans ~$3.7B agency-wide in hv_base, every FY 2016-2025 — it is the state's standard handling of inter-fund moves, cost allocations, and card spend, not a per-item accountability lapse. Recommendation for bondholders: DOWNWEIGHT this cluster and treat it as a data-transparency texture; the markers should be tuned so triple_gap/placeholder_payee do not fire on INTERNAL-rail or known card-aggregation vids. Low residual interest only because the convention masks vendor identity on genuinely large operating/capital outlays (max single item $4.88M, median $155K) — if a bondholder specifically wanted vendor-level visibility into DPS capital-equipment/fuel spend, that would require source records, but nothing in THIS data indicates a review-worthy anomaly.

agencyVerdicts.data_explanation: The triple_gap/placeholder_payee markers are rail-determined, not item-determined. Across all 15,358 high-value SUPREME COURT rows in mart/hv_base.parquet, payee is populated on 100% of ACH (7,145) and WARRANT (482) rows and on 0% of PAYMENT (4,068), INTERNAL (2,213), and NULL-method (1,446) rows. The 622 flagged items are essentially all payment_method=INTERNAL (620) with payee N/A or NULL and transaction_description N/A or NULL — i.e. the internal-transfer / cost-allocation / grant-distribution rail that this checkbook export simply never populates payee or description for. So the markers are detecting WHICH disbursement rail carries payee text, not which transactions are genuinely anonymous or unaccountable. The appropriations are fully legible, ordinary judicial-branch lines: JUDICIAL STATE PRIVATE GRANTS-SUPREME (grant pass-throughs to courts), AUTOMATION and PROBATION CASE MANAGEMENT SYSTEM (court IT/cost allocation), JUDGES COMPENSATION, FOSTER CARE REVIEW BOARD, OPERATING LUMP SUM. The same appropriation lines (e.g. JUDICIAL STATE PRIVATE GRANTS-SUPREME) appear on both the named ACH rail and the placeholder INTERNAL rail, confirming these are the internal-distribution leg of the same programs. Items are spread evenly across all 9 fiscal years (2016-2025: 48-103/yr), a stable long-running convention rather than an anomaly. The period13 subset (85 items) is year-end period-13 close booking of the same grant/automation INTERNAL distributions — expected accounting timing. This is data-transparency texture (an export field-population gap on internal rails), not per-item missing accountability. NOT downgraded to explained_benign only because of the embedded peer_outlier subset (see overtaker_interest / note).
agencyVerdicts.note: VERDICT: mixed. The placeholder_payee/triple_gap/period13 markers are a systemic accounting convention — the SUPREME COURT checkbook only populates payee/description on ACH and WARRANT rails; INTERNAL/PAYMENT/NULL-method distribution, cost-allocation and grant-pass-through rows are structurally blank. These should be DOWNWEIGHTED/annotated as a transparency-texture data artifact, not treated as per-item missing accountability, and the markers should be fixed to condition on payment rail (suppress placeholder_payee when payment_method in INTERNAL/PAYMENT). HOWEVER, the cluster is not purely benign: a peer_outlier subset (15 items, robust_z 20-40 in agency-category, ~$30M, mostly OPERATING LUMP SUM APPROPRIATION distributions of $1.5-2.8M each plus large JUDICIAL STATE PRIVATE GRANTS-SUPREME and the PROBATION CASE MANAGEMENT SYSTEM line) is review-worthy on MAGNITUDE — these are genuinely large discretionary lump-sum/grant disbursements a bondholder would legitimately want to see, regardless of the structural blank payee. RECOMMENDATION: keep/route the peer_outlier large lump-sums (the real signal is amount, not the missing payee); downweight the remaining ~600 routine INTERNAL grant/automation/cost-allocation rows as convention. Caveat applied: contract_number is 0% populated agency-wide so any no-contract marker is uninformative here.

agencyVerdicts.agency: DEPT OF EMERGENCY AND MILITARY AFFAIRS
agencyVerdicts.data_explanation: The 'triple_gap' and 'placeholder_payee' leads are a systemic internal-accounting convention, not missing accountability. Every flagged row shares an identical structural signature: vid=NULL, payee='N/A', transaction_description='N/A' (74 of 84) or NULL (10), and an INTERNAL/PAYMENT/NULL rail rather than ACH/WARRANT. Critically this same texture is agency-wide, not confined to the flagged subset: across ALL DEMA high-value rows, 2,857 of 6,486 (44% by count, ~$580M / 33% by dollars) carry an N/A or NULL payee on the INTERNAL/PAYMENT rail. So 'placeholder_payee' fires on a data-population gap that pervades the agency, and 'triple_gap' is its co-occurrence with the NULL-vid/manual-rail caveats (caveats 1 and 2) — none of which individually indicate a per-item concern. The Border Security program proves these are appropriation-side fund-loading/allocation entries that mirror real disbursements rather than separate untraceable payments: for BORDER SECURITY FENCE AND TECHNOLOGIES the placeholder INTERNAL rows ($116M, incl. the $92M and $6.8M capital-equipment items) sit alongside $231M of fully-named ACH disbursements to ASHBRITT INC (the 2022-23 shipping-container border-wall contractor) and Cochise County. For BORDER SECURITY FUND ALLOCATIONS the split is explicit — the placeholder INTERNAL/PAYMENT rows are the appropriation-into-fund moves while the named ACH/WARRANT rows are the actual outflows to vendors/counties. The remainder (CAMP NAVAJO, MILITARY AFFAIRS, MA FEDERAL FUNDING - ARMY/AIR NATL GUARD, ADMINISTRATION) are routine federal-match and base-operations cost-allocation/inter-fund entries on the same convention. Verdict is explained_benign rather than false_positive_marker because the markers are firing accurately on a real transparency-texture gap (no payee/description on internal moves) worth annotating at the agency level, and the underlying money IS traceable through the parallel named-vendor disbursements — it is just not carried on these internal allocation rows.
agencyVerdicts.note: Recommend annotating/downweighting both markers for this agency rather than treating items individually. Files: /Users/moeasnani/Documents/Openbooks/mart/tier_detail.parquet (flagged 84 items, all vid=NULL/payee=N/A/desc=N/A on INTERNAL rail) and /Users/moeasnani/Documents/Openbooks/mart/hv_base.parquet (agency-wide context: 44% of rows share the placeholder texture; named ASHBRITT INC border-fence ACH disbursements total ~$189M, BANNER HEALTH ~$505M COVID-era). One genuine-review residual worth a single targeted question to DEMA (not per-line): the $92M+ BORDER SECURITY FENCE capital-equipment INTERNAL allocation is a large discretionary, politically prominent line — confirming it reconciles cleanly to the named ASHBRITT/county outflows would close the loop, but the data already shows the matching named disbursements, so this is low-priority confirmation, not a finding.

agencyVerdicts.agency: EARLY CHILDHOOD DEVELOP AND HEALTH BOARD
agencyVerdicts.data_explanation: All 357 flagged items (214 triple_gap + 357 placeholder_payee, ~$105.6M) share one structural signature: placeholder payee (255 'N/A' / 102 NULL), placeholder/NULL transaction_description, NULL contract_name and contract_number, NULL vid, and payment_method='INTERNAL'. The placeholder payee is not an accountability gap specific to this agency; it is a system-wide accounting convention. Across the whole Arizona checkbook, the INTERNAL payment rail carries a placeholder (N/A/NULL) payee in essentially 100% of rows in every agency (AHCCCS 11,134/11,134; Corrections 7,722/7,722; DPS 4,831/4,831; Lottery 3,798/3,798; this agency 1,195/1,195). INTERNAL denotes inter-fund moves, cost allocations, payroll and transfers booked inside the accounting system, not disbursements to an external party — so there is no external payee to name. Within this agency the contrast is clean: its 163 named grantees (Valley of the Sun United Way, Southwest Human Development, ASU, U of A, Maricopa County, Navajo Nation, etc.) are all paid on real external rails (ACH $870M, WARRANT $237M); placeholder-payee rows appear ONLY on INTERNAL/PAYMENT/NULL rails and include PERSONAL SERVICES payroll ($40M) and TRANSFERS OUT ($22M), both routinely name-redacted. The flagged values are recurring identical amounts repeating across many fiscal years (e.g. $526,300 x14 spanning FY2019-2024; $404,760 x9; $336,684 x9; $719,245 booked annually), the signature of scheduled fund-administration / cost-allocation entries rather than discrete discretionary contract payments. The three markers (triple_gap, placeholder_payee, manual_rail_disc) are not independent corroboration: they all re-detect the same single fact (INTERNAL rail => placeholder payee => no contract), so the layered 'triple gap' inflates the appearance of risk without adding information. This is a data-transparency texture (internal-accounting convention), not genuinely missing accountability. DOWNWEIGHT/annotate; not a per-item review concern.
agencyVerdicts.note: Agency = First Things First (AZ Early Childhood Development and Health Board). Caveat: the benign read rests on the INTERNAL-rail convention; the underlying internal entries (cost allocations vs. fund administration) cannot be distinguished from this dataset because description/payee/contract are all suppressed on INTERNAL rows. If a reviewer wants assurance, the right ask is a one-time reconciliation of the recurring INTERNAL amounts (e.g. the $526,300 and $404,760 series) to their fund-administration source documents at the source-system level, not a per-transaction checkbook review. Recommended marker fix: suppress placeholder_payee/triple_gap/manual_rail_disc when payment_method='INTERNAL' (system-wide), since that combination is mechanically guaranteed and fires on routine inter-fund accounting across every agency.

agencyVerdicts.data_explanation: All 157 flagged items are a structural data-transparency texture, not missing-accountability events. The markers misfired on a systemic convention. (1) 100% have payee = 'N/A' or NULL, 99% (155/157) have transaction_description = 'N/A'/NULL, and 100% have NULL contract_number. So placeholder_payee, triple_gap (null-vendor + manual-rail + discretionary), and the offcontract component all fire by construction on every row -- they encode the absence of a checkbook payee field, not an anomaly. (2) The convention is agency-wide, not specific to the flagged subset: across ALL DOR high-value rows (hv_base) placeholder-payee transactions total ~$323M over 2,321 rows -- roughly half of DOR's entire high-value spend -- and the placeholder appears across every payment rail (PAYMENT, INTERNAL, NULL). 'N/A' (1,481 rows / $167M) and NULL (840 rows / $156M) are DOR's two single largest 'payees', dwarfing every real named vendor. The real counterparties for these dollars ARE in the data on named-payee rows (FAST ENTERPRISES, GCOM SOFTWARE, ASR ANALYTICS, VALORIT, GUIDESOFT, ORACLE, CDW-G, etc., per hv_base), confirming the payee simply lives in the encumbrance/contract system rather than the checkbook payee field. (3) agency_benford fires on 100% (157/157) of items -- an agency-level statistic stapled onto every row that adds no per-item signal. (4) ~78% (123/157) move on the INTERNAL/manual rail, exactly how inter-fund cost allocations and lump-sum operating distributions are booked. (5) The amounts are recurring identical scheduled allocations, the opposite of discretionary one-offs: $3,346,300 in FY24+FY25, $2,376,100 in FY16+FY17, $1,500,000 four times in FY16, $121,300 seven times across FY19-21, $103,900 seven times across FY17-18. (6) Appropriations are coherent operating/IT buckets: OPERATING LUMP SUM APPROPRIATION (internal cost allocation), INTEGRATED TAX SYSTEM MODERNIZATION and BRITS OPERATIONAL SUPPORT (the documented multi-year DOR tax-system replacement), TPT INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY, UNCLAIMED PROPERTY ADMIN. Per the team's READ-don't-infer rule, the descriptions/appropriations/category/rail all read as routine internal-accounting convention.
agencyVerdicts.note: True verdict is FALSE_POSITIVE_MARKER (not offered in the enum, so coded as explained_benign): fix the markers rather than escalate items. placeholder_payee/triple_gap fire on ~half of DOR's high-value checkbook (~$323M / 2,321 rows) because the agency systemically omits the checkbook payee/description field on internal-rail cost allocations and lump-sum operating distributions -- a data-transparency artifact, not a per-item accountability gap. The genuine vendors (FAST ENTERPRISES, GCOM, ASR ANALYTICS, VALORIT, ORACLE, etc.) are recorded elsewhere in the data. agency_benford fires on 100% of rows and should be demoted from per-item scoring to an agency-level flag. Suggested fix: suppress placeholder_payee/triple_gap when an item's appropriation maps to a known internal cost-allocation bucket and a named vendor exists on the matching encumbrance; join checkbook rows to the encumbrance/contract payee before scoring. The whole_dollar/round_100k/peer_outlier add-ons attach to recurring identical budgeted allocations (e.g. $3,346,300 repeated in consecutive years), which is the signature of a scheduled distribution, not a discretionary one-off. The only legitimate lead is the data-quality gap itself -- ~$323M of DOR high-value spend lacking a checkbook-visible payee -- a transparency finding for the State, not evidence of wrongdoing in any individual transaction.

agencyVerdicts.data_explanation: All 161 flagged DOE items are INTERNAL-rail postings (payment_method='INTERNAL') with payee='N/A'/NULL, transaction_description='N/A'/NULL, and vendor_id_code='00000000000000000000' (all-zeros system placeholder) or NULL. The placeholder_payee marker is NOT DOE-specific: it fires across 70 agencies on 15,047 items totaling ~$8.9B (DES $1.79B, ADOT $1.6B, DCS, ADOA, AHCCCS all larger). The all-zeros vendor_id_code is the export's standard placeholder (11,916 rows globally). DOE's $79.8M is a small, proportionate slice ranking ~14th. triple_gap is mechanical-by-construction: its three legs (null-vendor = the all-zeros placeholder, manual-rail = INTERNAL method, discretionary) are all consequences of the same fact — the row is an internal-rail posting whose payee is suppressed in the export — not three independent risk signals. The appropriations are all legitimate named education programs (Education for the Disadvantaged/Title I, Special Education, Prop 301 Accountability & Achievement Testing, AZELLA/ELA assessment, Child Nutrition, ESA, School Safety, Adult Education), consistent with inter-fund moves, cost allocations, and chargebacks (category2 dominated by OTHER PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES / IT SERVICES; AGENCY CHARGEBACKS appropriation present). Per the team's READ-don't-infer rule there is no descriptive text to read (uniformly N/A), and the null payee/vendor fields are fully explained by the internal-rail publishing convention rather than any missing-accountability event.
agencyVerdicts.note: Downweight/annotate, not a DOE accountability finding. The placeholder_payee + triple_gap markers are essentially structural texture here (suppressed-payee INTERNAL rail), systemic across 70 agencies and $8.9B statewide. Recommend the marker team treat null-payee + all-zeros vendor_id_code + INTERNAL method as a known export convention and suppress triple_gap when all three legs derive from that single condition. Residual: a small minority of items also carry SECONDARY markers worth a one-line GL cross-reference rather than a flag — notably the $7.0M FY2023 and $3.9M FY2025 'Education for the Disadvantaged' items (also peer_outlier) and several period13/june_round year-end round-dollar internal moves ($2.0M-$2.5M on Prop 301 testing, AZELLA, School Safety, Special Ed Voucher). These large/round/year-end-on-a-discretionary-federal-passthrough entries are explainable as Title I subgrant/assessment-contract true-ups but are the one texture a bondholder might want annotated; that keeps the cluster from being a pure false_positive_marker. Net: explained_benign with a systemic data-transparency convention; light annotation on the ~6-8 outlier/year-end items.

redteam.est_false_positive_share: ~29% of round-family fires (9,061 rows) are benign-by-structure on payee/appropriation alone; ~47% by dollars. Within the STRONG markers only (round_1m/round_100k/june_round, excluding whole_dollar), it is worse: ~49% benign-by-structure, with postage replenishment alone = 24% of fires. whole_dollar is weakest: its 13.2% universal base rate (share of ALL high-value rows that are whole-dollar) means it adds little lift anywhere. A conservative set of guardrails suppresses 21.4% of fires (~$2.6B) with zero observed loss of genuine signal.

fp_patterns.pattern: POSTAGE METER REPLENISHMENT - agencies fund Pitney Bowes / USPS / United Business Mail / presort meters in inherently round top-ups ($1M, $1.5M, $2M, $900K). 226 rows within strong round markers (24% of them); 278 rows across the full family up to $2M each. Compounds with person_name_payee ('Bowes' reads as a surname) and offcontract/peer_outlier, inflating risk_score.
fp_patterns.example: PITNEY BOWES <- DEPT OF TRANSPORTATION, appropriation 'POSTAGE FUND' / 'OPERATING LUMP SUM', $2,000,000 (markers: round_1m,peer_outlier,offcontract,person_name_payee)
fp_patterns.why_benign: Postage meters are prepaid; the dollar amount is a discretionary round top-up of a debit account, not a priced transaction. Roundness is mechanical, not negotiated. Vendor and appropriation are unambiguous.

fp_patterns.pattern: PSPRS / ASRS UNFUNDED-LIABILITY PAYDOWNS - year-end (june_round) lump pension contributions, often the agency paying its own retirement system or appropriation literally named '...UNFUNDED LIABILITY REDUCTION'. 27 strong-marker rows = $1.365B, dominating the family by dollars (e.g. ten $99M FY2021 june_round payments).
fp_patterns.example: PUBLIC SAFETY PERSONNEL RETIREMENT SYSTEM, appropriation 'PSPRS DPS UNFUNDED LIABILITY REDUCTION', payee PUBLIC SAFETY PERSONNEL RETIREMENT, $99,000,000 (markers: june_round)
fp_patterns.why_benign: Pension is explicitly benign-by-structure. Unfunded-liability paydowns are statutorily scheduled, round by design (legislature appropriates round dollar figures), and paid to PSPRS/ASRS.

fp_patterns.pattern: LOTTERY PRIZE-FUND / BANK TRANSFERS - Lottery Commission moves prize and operating funds to BANK OF AMERICA - AZ in round amounts ($2M-$90M). 60 strong-marker rows = $263M; 1,081 whole_dollar rows tied to lottery/BofA = $490M.
fp_patterns.example: LOTTERY COMMISSION, appropriation 'PRIZE FUND', payee BANK OF AMERICA - AZ, $90,000,000 (markers: round_1m)
fp_patterns.why_benign: Lottery->bank is named in the benign-by-structure list. These are scheduled cash sweeps to the banking partner, not vendor procurement; roundness reflects fund transfers.

fp_patterns.pattern: ADOT RIGHT-OF-WAY LAND ACQUISITION via title/escrow agent - SECURITY TITLE AGENCY (222 whole_dollar rows, $216M) settles freeway land purchases; deals settle in whole-dollar/round-ish amounts on FEDERAL AID / M-RARF freeway appropriations.
fp_patterns.example: SECURITY TITLE AGENCY INC <- DEPT OF TRANSPORTATION, appropriation 'FEDERAL AID' / 'M-RARF CONSTRUCT FREEWAYS', $28,425,000 (markers: whole_dollar,peer_outlier,no_contract_named,offcontract,nonstd_rail)
fp_patterns.why_benign: Land condemnation/purchase prices are appraisal-driven and routinely whole-dollar; the title agent is a pass-through escrow, not a discretionary vendor. Concentration is real but whole-dollar is not the reason.

fp_patterns.pattern: RISK-MANAGEMENT / INSURANCE-CLAIM SETTLEMENTS - ADOA 'RISK MANAGEMENT LOSSES AND PREMIUMS' / 'CONSTRUCTION INSURANCE CLAIMS' paid in round settlement amounts. 74 strong-marker rows = $79M.
fp_patterns.example: DEPT OF ADMINISTRATION, appropriation 'RISK MANAGEMENT LOSSES AND PREMIUMS', $10,800,000 (markers: june_round)
fp_patterns.why_benign: Legal/insurance settlements are negotiated to round figures by nature; the round amount is the settlement, not a procurement anomaly.

fp_patterns.pattern: DOCUMENTED MAJOR CONTRACTED PER-DIEM / CAPITATION (softer) - GEO private-prison 'PRIVATE PRISON PER DIEM' and NaphCare/Centurion/Corizon 'INMATE HEALTH CARE CONTRACTED' disbursements land on round/whole-dollar drawdowns; AHCCCS Prop-204 admin similarly.
fp_patterns.example: GEO CORRECTIONS AND DETENTION LLC <- DEPT OF CORRECTIONS, appropriation 'PRIVATE PRISON PER DIEM', $5,000,000 (markers: round_1m)
fp_patterns.why_benign: These are large documented contracts; round monthly/periodic drawdowns are administrative, not negotiated-price anomalies. Concentration-worthy but the round amount is not itself a red flag.

recommended_suppressions: POSTAGE (highest-yield, zero false-suppression observed): suppress round_* / whole_dollar when upper(payee) LIKE 'PITNEY BOWES%' OR upper(payee) LIKE '%POSTAL SERVICE%' OR upper(payee)='UNITED BUSINESS MAIL' OR upper(payee) LIKE '%PRESORT%' OR upper(appropriation_1_name) LIKE '%POSTAGE%'. Removes 278 rows (24% of strong-marker fires). Bonus: also stop person_name_payee firing on 'PITNEY BOWES'.

PENSION PAYDOWN (scope tightly, do NOT blanket-exclude the pension agency): suppress when upper(appropriation_1_name) LIKE '%UNFUNDED LIABILITY%' OR upper(appropriation_1_name) LIKE '%PSPRS%' OR upper(payee) LIKE '%PUBLIC SAFETY PERSONNEL RETIREMENT%' OR upper(payee) LIKE '%STATE RETIREMENT SYSTEM%'. Deliberately leave ASRS 'INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT FEES' (72 rows, paid to outside managers) IN scope - those are the one place round pension-agency spend may merit a look.

LOTTERY: suppress when agency LIKE '%LOTTERY%' OR upper(payee) LIKE 'BANK OF AMERICA%' OR upper(appropriation_1_name) LIKE '%PRIZE%'. (Aligns with the existing benign-by-structure rule; the round category guard currently lets these through to whole_dollar.)
RISK / INSURANCE SETTLEMENTS: suppress when upper(appropriation_1_name) LIKE '%RISK MANAGEMENT%' OR LIKE '%INSURANCE CLAIM%' OR LIKE '%LOSSES AND PREMIUMS%'.

ADOT RIGHT-OF-WAY LAND: downweight (not hard-kill) when upper(payee) LIKE '%TITLE AGENCY%' OR upper(payee) LIKE '%TITLE INSURANCE%' OR upper(appropriation_1_name) LIKE '%RIGHT OF WAY%' (keep as concentration lead, drop the round/whole-dollar contribution to risk_score).

WHOLE_DOLLAR globally: stop using whole_dollar as a standalone or score-boosting marker - its base rate is 13.2% of ALL high-value rows. Require it to co-fire with >=1 independent strong marker (sole_source, new_vendor_large+new_vendor_dominant, vendor_dependency, peer_outlier with robust_z_in_agencycat above threshold) before it contributes to risk_score; otherwise treat as descriptive metadata only.

DOCUMENTED-CONTRACT softener: downweight round_* when category1='PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES' AND appropriation in ('PRIVATE PRISON PER DIEM','INMATE HEALTH CARE CONTRACTED') OR appropriation LIKE '%CAPITATION%'/'%PROP 204%' - keep as concentration lead but remove the roundness boost.
redteam.notes: DISCIPLINE-COMPLIANT: every classification above is read from the data (payee + appropriation_1_name + category1 + agency), never inferred. KEEP the marker - after the guardrails the genuine residual is clearly review-worthy and round amounts add real lift there: e.g. round_100k+sole_source+new_vendor_dominant $12.5M to ACTIVE RESOURCE MANAGEMENT (Water Banking Authority); round_1m+sole_source+new_vendor_dominant $7M to FONDOMONTE ARIZONA LLC (Saudi-owned groundwater/alfalfa lessee, State & Local Fiscal Recovery) - exactly a bondholder lead; round_1m+sole_source $4M AshBritt (Border Security Fund); $7M Holocaust Education to a short-tenure new vendor. Marker-interaction findings worth fixing at the marker layer, not just suppressing: (1) PITNEY BOWES double-misfires - it trips both round_* AND person_name_payee; the postage guard should feed the person_name_payee allowlist too. (2) june_round is the highest-dollar but most concentrated round marker - ~$1.18B of its $1.18B is FY2021 PSPRS paydowns; almost a single-purpose pension detector right now. (3) round markers ride alongside offcontract/no_contract_named constantly, but per the known caveat contract_number is ~2-6% populated, so that co-fire is not corroboration. Quant summary: full family 9,061 fires / $6.45B; ~29% benign-by-structure by count (~47% by $). Recommended guardrails suppress 1,942 fires (21.4%, $2.63B) with no genuine items lost in inspection; the postage + whole_dollar-gating changes are the highest-value, lowest-risk fixes.

redteam.est_false_positive_share: ~95-97% of fired groups are benign-by-structure (96.8% of the 220 tier_detail dup lead-groups; 96.4% of the 1,876 hv_base dup groups). Only ~3-5% (67 hv_base groups / 7 tier groups) carry genuine signal.

fp_patterns.pattern: Same disbursement document split into accounting/distribution lines. The two 'duplicate' rows share the SAME transaction_reference_id (GAX/PRC doc number), SAME EFT/PRC transaction_id root, SAME invoice_number, dup_day_span=0 — they differ ONLY in the accounting-line index in the transaction_id suffix. One invoice is booked across 2+ object/fund lines, each surfacing as a row. 97.8% of sameday dup groups share a single transaction_reference_id; 98.7% share one-or-null invoice.
fp_patterns.example: SANDATA TECHNOLOGIES LLC / AHCCCS FY2021: invoice 0268381-IN, doc GAXHCAADM210000055, two rows of $196,399.76 with transaction_ids ...___1___5___2021 and ...___1___6___2021 (line 5 vs line 6 of the same EFT). 28 such identical-amount lines across the year.
fp_patterns.why_benign: It is ONE real EFT payment of an invoice decomposed into ledger lines, not a repeated payment. No money left twice.

fp_patterns.pattern: Medicaid managed-care capitation posting across a 1-day settlement window (drives in_dup_diffday). Same health plan, same exact monthly capitation amount, same transaction_reference_id (GAXCMHCAPRG...) posting on two consecutive days. 92% of diffday rows / 99.6% of diffday dollars are AHCCCS; 93% of diffday groups share one ref id.
fp_patterns.example: MERCY CARE PLAN / AHCCCS: $35,857,549.22 on 2017-10-04 and 2017-10-05 under one doc GAXHCAPMMS18041289; HEALTH NET ACCESS / ARIZONA PHYSICIANS IPA / BANNER UNIVERSITY FAMILY CARE all show identical pairs day-apart in FY2025.
fp_patterns.why_benign: Explicit AHCCCS->health-plan capitation (benign-by-structure caveat #4); the 'two days' is a posting-window artifact of a single capitation document, not a second payment.

fp_patterns.pattern: Pension paydown / debt-service principal split into multiple same-day lines. A single large appropriation is booked as N identical lines under one GAX doc (dup_rows=10), or a bond principal is split into invoice lines '1PRINCIPALS'/'2PRINCIPALS'.
fp_patterns.example: PSPRS / PUBLIC SAFETY PERSONNEL RETIREMENT SYSTEM: 10 lines of $99,000,000 on 2021-06-30 under doc GAXRSA210000002854 (suffixes A1..K1). BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON TRUST / School Facilities Board: 2x $69,840,000 invoices 1PRINCIPALS/2PRINCIPALS.
fp_patterns.why_benign: PSPRS pension (caveat #4) and bond-trustee debt service (caveat #4); identical lines are a single scheduled disbursement.

fp_patterns.pattern: Interstate / intergovernmental installment batch run on one day. Sequential weekly installments to another state agency or a fixed-rate recurring contract, all the same per-period amount, posted same day under sequentially numbered docs.
fp_patterns.example: STATE OF KANSAS / DEPT OF ECONOMIC SECURITY: ten $25,833.50 lines (invoices 0120-1120) on 2021-01-22 under doc GAXDEA2UI00348 — an interstate UI benefit-recovery transfer schedule. 22ND CENTURY TECHNOLOGIES / Homeland Security: identical $112,560 monthly IT-staffing across FY2023-2024 (dup_diffday_disc fired on a fixed-price recurring contract).
fp_patterns.why_benign: Intergovernmental transfer / scheduled fixed-rate disbursement (caveats #3, #4); equal amounts are the contract rate, not a duplicate.

recommended_suppressions: SUPPRESS when all rows in the dup group share ONE transaction_reference_id (same GAX/PRC document). This alone kills 97.8% of sameday and 93% of diffday groups. SQL: in the dup signature definition, add HAVING count(DISTINCT transaction_reference_id) > 1 (only flag when the identical amount is paid under genuinely DIFFERENT documents).

SUPPRESS same-document line splits via the transaction_id root: derive doc_root = regexp_replace(transaction_id,'___[0-9]+___[0-9]+___[0-9]+___[0-9]+$','') and require count(DISTINCT doc_root) > 1 within the group. Lines that differ only in the trailing accounting-line index collapse to one root and are dropped.

EXCLUDE agency = 'AHCCCS' from the diffday duplicate marker entirely (or gate it on dup_day_span<=1 being benign): Medicaid capitation is 92% of diffday rows and 99.6% of diffday dollars and is benign-by-structure. Equivalently, suppress when category/appropriation indicates capitation/managed-care.

EXCLUDE benign-by-structure payee classes from BOTH duplicate markers: payee ILIKE '%RETIREMENT SYSTEM%' (PSPRS/ASRS pension), payee ILIKE '%TRUST COMPANY%' or '%BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON%' (bond/debt-service trustees), and intergovernmental payees (other states, counties, cities, universities/colleges, US Treasury/HHS). Combined with the single-ref rule this raises suppression to 96.4% while leaving 67 genuine groups.
For diffday specifically, require dup_day_span between 2 and ~25 days AND distinct transaction_reference_id before flagging — a 0-1 day span on one doc is a posting-window split, not a re-payment.

After suppressions, KEEP and prioritize groups where count(DISTINCT transaction_reference_id) > 1 with a commercial (non-IGA) payee — these are the genuine 'identical exact amount paid under separate documents' leads (e.g. PUBLIC CONSULTING GROUP, MICROSOFT, DELOITTE, ACCENTURE, CORIZON, DIONA, INEXL CONSULTING, NAPHCARE, and several round-amount payments to redacted individuals at DCS/DES/Housing).
redteam.notes: Mechanism (verified from mart/hv_base.parquet, never warehouse.duckdb): the 'duplicate' fires because Arizona's ledger decomposes one EFT/warrant into multiple accounting-distribution lines that share payee+amount+day. The marker reads each line as a repeated payment. The decisive forensic key is transaction_reference_id (the GAX/PRC document number): when all 'duplicate' rows share it, no money moved twice. 97.8% of sameday groups and 93% of diffday groups fail this test (single shared doc). dup_diffday is almost entirely AHCCCS Medicaid capitation posting across a 1-2 day settlement window. The classic batch/installment FP the brief asked us to find is concretely present: SANDATA (28x $196,399.76 invoice-line splits), STATE OF KANSAS (weekly UI-recovery installments 0120-1120 in one run), 22ND CENTURY TECHNOLOGIES (fixed monthly IT-staffing rate), PSPRS ($990M pension paydown as 10 lines). The marker is NOT worthless: after the single-ref + AHCCCS + pension/trustee/IGA guardrails, 67 hv_base groups survive as legitimate genuine_review leads (same vendor, identical exact amount, SEPARATE GAX documents) — the real split-payment / double-billing signal the marker exists to find. Overall verdict for the family: mixed, dominated by false_positive_marker (line-split structural misfire) with a small but real genuine_review residue; keep the marker but add the suppressions. Tier_detail rows for this family appear as identical aggregated pairs (e.g. SHI/AHCCCS 2025 two rows of $663,784) because tier_detail rolls the underlying split lines up; the dup_distinct_txn/dup_rows/in_dup_signature columns live in hv_base. Relevant files: /Users/moeasnani/Documents/Openbooks/mart/tier_detail.parquet and /Users/moeasnani/Documents/Openbooks/mart/hv_base.parquet (key cols: transaction_reference_id, transaction_id, invoice_number, dup_distinct_txn, dup_rows, dup_day_span, in_dup_signature, in_dup_diffday).

redteam.est_false_positive_share: ~55-60% of firings by count (626 of 1,053 rows; ~$1.5B of $2.6B) are benign-by-structure

fp_patterns.pattern: Employee health-benefits program administrator treated as a discretionary sole-source vendor. The vendor_dependency marker excludes AID/TRANSFERS/DEBT categories but NOT the self-insured state employee benefits plan, which books under OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES at a high agency share.
fp_patterns.example: UNITED HEALTHCARE at DEPT OF ADMINISTRATION - 176 rows, $1.11B, appropriation_1_name = 'NON APPROPRIATED BENEFITS PROGRAM'. This is the State of Arizona self-insured employee health plan administered through ADOA, a scheduled benefits disbursement, not arm's-length procurement.
fp_patterns.why_benign: Largest single concentration item in the whole family ($1.1B). It is a statutory non-appropriated employee-benefits program; the 'sole provider' is the third-party administrator for state-employee health coverage. Per the brief, scheduled benefit disbursements are benign-by-structure.

fp_patterns.pattern: Lottery -> bank prize-fund settlements and the state treasury/banking concentration. The brief's caveat (4) names 'Lottery (-> banks)' and debt/treasury as benign-by-structure, and marker 1 (sole_source) word-boundary-suppresses banks -- but marker 2 (vendor_dependency) INTENTIONALLY retains banks, so they fire as 'dependency'. For a bondholder red-team these are pure structural exposure, not review items.
fp_patterns.example: BANK OF AMERICA - AZ at LOTTERY COMMISSION - 414 rows, $363M, appropriation_1_name = 'PRIZE FUND' (Lottery prize/banking settlement). U S BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION at STATEWIDE SYSTEMS PROCESSING - 6 rows, $12.6M, share 0.74 (state banking/treasury concentration). All 420 fire vendor_dependency ONLY, zero sole_source.
fp_patterns.why_benign: Lottery prize-fund disbursements route through the Lottery's banking partner by structural necessity; statewide-systems-processing is the state's own treasury/banking rail. Both are named benign-by-structure in the brief (Lottery->banks, debt-service/treasury).

fp_patterns.pattern: Intergovernmental payee whose NAME does not literally equal the agency, so the existing self-payment / university suppression misses it. The guard suppresses payee=agency, 'STATE OF ARIZONA%', and (payee LIKE '%UNIVERSITY%' AND agency LIKE '%UNIVERSITY%'); but a university paid by a NON-university agency, or one state agency paying another, slips through.
fp_patterns.example: ARIZONA COMMERCE AUTHORITY at OFFICE OF ECONOMIC OPPORTUNITY (20 rows, $12.9M, state-agency-to-state-agency IGA); ARIZONA STATE UNIVERSITY at BOARD OF REGENTS and UNIVERSITY OF ARIZONA (FINANCIAL SERVICES OFFICE) at GEOLOGICAL SURVEY (agency side lacks 'UNIVERSITY' so the both-sides guard fails); DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES at PERSONNEL BOARD (federal pass-through); SHARLOT HALL HISTORICAL SOCIETY at PRESCOTT HISTORICAL SOCIETY.
fp_patterns.why_benign: These are IGAs / intergovernmental pass-throughs to universities, other state agencies, and federal HHS -- explicitly called out as 'usually legitimate IGAs/pass-throughs' in the brief's caveat (3).

recommended_suppressions: Suppress the employee-benefits program: add to BOTH markers NOT (upper(appropriation_1_name) LIKE '%BENEFITS PROGRAM%' OR upper(appropriation_1_name) LIKE '%NON APPROPRIATED BENEFITS%'). Removes 176 rows / $1.11B (UNITED HEALTHCARE @ ADOA), zero collateral -- verified the entire BENEFITS-PROGRAM block is this one structural vendor.

Suppress Lottery/treasury bank prize-fund in vendor_dependency (marker 2 currently retains banks by design): add NOT (upper(appropriation_1_name) LIKE '%PRIZE FUND%' OR (regexp_matches(upper(payee),'(^| )(U S BANK|BANK OF AMERICA|WELLS FARGO|JPMORGAN|BNY MELLON|NORTHERN TRUST)( |,|$)') AND agency IN ('LOTTERY COMMISSION','STATEWIDE SYSTEMS PROCESSING'))). Reuse the SAME word-boundary regex already in marker 1 (avoids the BANNER-HEALTH/'BANK' substring trap). Removes 420 rows / $363M.

Broaden the intergovernmental/self-payment guard so payee-name need not equal agency-name. Add: NOT (regexp_matches(upper(payee),'(^| )(DEPARTMENT OF|DEPT OF|UNIVERSITY|BOARD OF REGENTS|COMMUNITY COLLEGE|COMMERCE AUTHORITY|HISTORICAL SOCIETY|HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES)( |,|$)')). Word-boundary anchored, not substring. Removes ~30 rows / $14.6M of confirmed IGA/university/federal pass-throughs while leaving private vendors untouched.

Do NOT add any generic 'small single-purpose commission' suppressor: the redistricting-commission law firms (Ballard Spahr, Snell & Wilmer, Osborn Maledon) and PETSMART CHARITIES @ Spay/Neuter Committee (share 0.99) are the predicate's own named caveat cases and are legitimately review-worthy at low severity -- keep them as dependency leads, do not auto-suppress.
redteam.notes: VERDICT: keep the marker -- after the three structural guards, the surviving core is exactly what a bondholder/auditor wants: NAPHCARE @ Dept of Corrections ($795M inmate healthcare sole-source), ASHBRITT @ Emergency/Military Affairs ($194M 'BORDER SECURITY' emergency contract, also new-vendor), OFF MADISON AVE @ Office of Tourism ($51M statewide marketing/PR dependency), plus CARAHSOFT, construction at the Legislature/Senate, and redistricting law firms. The marker's existing guards are already strong (word-boundary bank regex explicitly unit-tested against BANNER HEALTH; vid IS NOT NULL prevents FY2019/2020 thin-year firing; entitlement categories excluded). The three residual FP classes are all benign-by-STRUCTURE rather than substring traps. Biggest single fix by dollars is the UNITED HEALTHCARE employee-benefits program ($1.1B, 42% of family dollars). Biggest by COUNT is BANK OF AMERICA Lottery prize fund (414 rows). All three guards verified non-overlapping with the genuine core. Scope note: the bank guard belongs in marker 2 (vendor_dependency) specifically, since marker 1 (sole_source) already word-boundary-suppresses banks and 0 of the 420 bank firings carried sole_source. The 'offcontract'/'no_contract_named' co-markers on these rows are noise (contract_number ~2-6% populated) and should not add weight."

redteam.est_false_positive_share: ~70-80% in FY2021 (376/469 flagged vendors are old resurfacing payees); ~45% across the whole family (449/1006 flagged vendor-years had a prior-year payee); only ~17% in FY2023-2025. Net: roughly 65-70% of all current new_vendor fires are structural false positives, almost all concentrated in the FY2021 transition year.

fp_patterns.pattern: FY2021 vid-gap + vid-renumbering artifact: vendor_first_year is computed off vid, but vid is 100% NULL in FY2019-2020 (97,422 + 105,519 rows, vendor_id_code also 100% NULL) AND the vid scheme was re-minted in 2021 (new 'IV%' prefix = 0 rows in 2016-2018 but 25,926 rows in 2021; old 'PZ%' scheme collapses from ~28k/yr to 7k). So an established vendor gets a brand-new vid in 2021 and is mislabeled first-seen-2021. Match-by-payee proves 376/469 (80%) of FY2021 'new' vendors already received >=$25K in FY2016-2020.
fp_patterns.example: MEDIMPACT HEALTHCARE SYSTEMS INC: vid PZ9000007853 in 2016-2018 (~$180-193M/yr), NULL in 2019-2020 (~$192-221M/yr), then re-minted to IV0000017438 in 2021 (~$224M/yr) -> fired new_vendor_dominant+new_vendor_large. Identical for BLUE CROSS BLUE SHIELD OF AZ ($980M history) and GORDON-DARBY ($114M).
fp_patterns.why_benign: Same payee, same line of business, continuous multi-hundred-million-dollar spend straddling the NULL blackout. 'New' is purely an artifact of the vid going NULL in 2019-2020 and being re-keyed in 2021.

fp_patterns.pattern: Intergovernmental / IGA payees swept up by the same FY2021 artifact (35 of 469 flagged).
fp_patterns.example: ARIZONA STATE UNIVERSITY (vid IV0000000997) flagged new_vendor_dominant + new_vendor_large in 2021 despite $3.99B of prior-year history; also COLORADO RIVER INDIAN TRIBES.
fp_patterns.why_benign: Universities/tribes/counties are benign-by-structure IGAs and pass-throughs per the known caveats; ASU is one of the single largest recurring payees in the entire checkbook -- definitionally not a new vendor.

fp_patterns.pattern: Major established highway/prison contractors re-keyed in 2021 (40 of 469 flagged are construction).
fp_patterns.example: AMES CONSTRUCTION ($102M hist), FNF CONSTRUCTION ($452M), FISHER SAND AND GRAVEL ($141M), FANN CONTRACTING ($216M), PULICE CONSTRUCTION ($204M), CORECIVIC ($201M), CENTURION OF ARIZONA prison healthcare ($205M, fired short_tenure_vendor).
fp_patterns.why_benign: Decade-long ADOT/ADC vendors with $100M-$450M of pre-2021 spend. The 'no_contract_named/offcontract' co-fires are themselves weak (contract_number only ~2-6% populated), so they add no real corroboration.

fp_patterns.pattern: short_tenure_vendor mislabels vendors whose tenure only looks short because the 2019-2020 years are vid-NULL, breaking the years-active count.
fp_patterns.example: COFFMAN AMES JOINT VENTURE (hist from 2019, $121M) and SUNDT CONSTRUCTION fired short_tenure_vendor in 2021 despite continuous activity across the blackout.
fp_patterns.why_benign: vendor_years_active undercounts because the 2019-2020 contributions carry a NULL vid and cannot be attributed to the vendor, artificially compressing tenure.

recommended_suppressions: Payee-name resurfacing guard (single most effective fix): suppress any new_vendor_* fire when upper(trim(payee)) already received >=$25K in any prior fiscal year in hv_base. Implement as: NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM hv_base h WHERE upper(trim(h.payee))=upper(trim(t.payee)) AND h.fiscal_year < t.fiscal_year). This alone removes 376/469 FY2021 FPs (80%) while leaving the 93 genuinely-new FY2021 vendors and the FY2023-2025 signal intact.

Hard-exclude the FY2019->2021 vid discontinuity: do not let any vendor be first_seen=2021 on vid alone. Treat 2021 as a 'reset' boundary and require corroborating evidence (no prior payee match) before allowing new_vendor_* to fire for fiscal_year=2021. Equivalently, recompute vendor_first_year on a stable key (payee+vendor_id_code where present) rather than the re-minted vid.

Cross-scheme vid bridge: build a payee->vid crosswalk so a 2021 'IV%' vid that maps to a 2016-2018 'PZ%'/numeric vid for the same payee inherits the older first_year. This fixes the renumbering (IV% = 0 rows pre-2021, 25,926 in 2021).

Intergovernmental allowlist: never fire new_vendor_* for payees matching UNIVERSITY|COMMUNITY COLLEGE|COUNTY|CITY OF|TOWN OF|TRIBE|TREASURY|STATE OF|DISTRICT|AHCCCS health plans|PSPRS|ASRS -- benign-by-structure per the known caveats (35+ of the 469 FY2021 fires).
Do not count weak co-fires (offcontract / no_contract_named) as corroboration for new_vendor severity, since contract_number is only ~2-6% populated system-wide.
For short_tenure_vendor specifically: compute tenure from payee-level (or vendor_id_code-level) activity, not vid, so the 2019-2020 NULL years still count toward years-active.
redteam.notes: Verdict: KEEP the marker family but it is currently dominated by a single structural false positive. Mechanism is two compounding data artifacts, both confirmed from the data, not inferred: (1) FY2019 and FY2020 are 100% NULL vid AND 100% NULL vendor_id_code system-wide (the documented blackout), and (2) the vid scheme was renumbered in FY2021 -- the 'IV%' prefix has 0 rows in 2016-2018 and 25,926 rows in 2021 while the old 'PZ%' scheme collapses. Because vendor_first_year is derived from vid, every established vendor that straddled the blackout gets a fresh vid in 2021 and is mislabeled first-seen-2021. Distribution proves the concentration: of 982 flagged vendors, 469 (48%) have vendor_first_year=2021, and 3,893 of 5,548 flagged rows (70%) are FY2021; FY2019, 2020 and 2022 are entirely absent. Matching by payee name (which is NOT null in 2019-2020) shows 376/469 (80%) of FY2021 'new' vendors already had >=$25K prior history -- e.g. MedImpact, Blue Cross AZ, ASU ($3.99B), CoreCivic, Centurion, and five major ADOT contractors. The marker behaves acceptably from FY2023 on (only ~14-18% prior-payee match), so the fix is narrow: a payee-name resurfacing guard plus treating 2021 as a vid-reset boundary. After suppression, ~93 plausibly-genuine FY2021 new vendors remain (e.g. VIZIENT, SLEEP MANAGEMENT, PARADIGM LABORATORIES, PM INDUSTRIAL HOLDINGS -- all with NULL pre-2021 history), preserving the real signal. Key files: mart/tier_detail.parquet (fired_markers, vendor_first_appearance), mart/hv_base.parquet (vendor_first_year, vendor_years_active, vid prefixes by year).

redteam.est_false_positive_share: ~75-80% overall. period13_disc ~95% FP, period13 ~80% FP, june_round ~53% FP (structurally benign); only june_round retains a meaningful clean external signal (~47%, ~89 rows).

fp_patterns.pattern: period13_disc / period13 fire on NULL/N-A-payee INTERNAL-rail year-end accrual journal entries. The discretionary-category label is present but there is no readable counterparty and no cash rail, so the row is an unverifiable book true-up, not a payment. Of 192 period13_disc rows, 181 (94%) are payment_method=INTERNAL and ALL 192 (100%) have payee N/A or NULL; period13 is 76% INTERNAL and 97% null-payee. Violates the 'READ the payee, never infer' discipline because there is nothing to read.
fp_patterns.example: SUPREME COURT, appropriation 'AUTOMATION' / 'OPERATING LUMP SUM', payee=N/A, INTERNAL rail, 85 rows ~$30M, fp_canon=13 (posts Jul-Aug). Court IT/automation year-end accrual booked in the adjustment period.
fp_patterns.why_benign: Period-13 is the post-close adjustment period; these are GAAP accruals for goods/services received by Jun-30 but not yet invoiced, recorded as internal journal entries with no vendor assigned. Benign-by-structure book entries, expected and unverifiable as 'payments'.

fp_patterns.pattern: june_round fires on agency=payee self-payments (book transfers to own funds / pension self-pay). The arms-length filter present on the round_1m/round_100k markers was NOT applied to june_round, so same-entity round-dollar movements survive.
fp_patterns.example: PUBLIC SAFETY PERSONNEL RETIREMENT SYSTEM -> PUBLIC SAFETY PERSONNEL RETIREMENT SYSTEM, 11-12 identical round payments totaling ~$1.0B in June FY2021, appropriation 'PSPRS UNFUNDED LIABILITY', INTERNAL rail. (Plus ARIZONA COMMERCE AUTHORITY and ARIZONA STATE UNIVERSITY self-pays.)
fp_patterns.why_benign: Scheduled pension unfunded-liability paydown (PSPRS/ASRS family) is benign-by-structure per caveat 4. agency=payee means it is an intra-entity fund movement, not an arms-length disbursement. The marker's own guard text explicitly named this PSPRS case as low-suspicion but the suppression (payee<>agency) was never wired in.

fp_patterns.pattern: june_round / period13 fire on INTERNAL / JOURNAL-VOUCHER / COST-ALLOCATION rail rows. Across the whole yearend family 423/677 (62%) ride these non-cash internal rails, which the round-dollar markers exclude but the yearend markers do not.
fp_patterns.example: DEPT OF ADMINISTRATION 'OTHER EXTERNAL COMPUTER PROCESSING', payee N/A, INTERNAL rail, ~$18M june_round; numerous DOA/DES/DHS internal cost-allocation true-ups.
fp_patterns.why_benign: Internal/journal-voucher/cost-allocation rails are book entries (chargebacks, indirect-cost allocation, fund sweeps), not arms-length vendor payments. Round dollars on these rails are an accounting artifact of allocation, not a discretionary procurement decision.

fp_patterns.pattern: period13_disc score inflation via co-firing with placeholder_payee + triple_gap. The SAME structural void (null payee on a book entry) trips three markers at once, so risk_score compounds for a single benign cause.
fp_patterns.example: SUPREME COURT N/A payee rows carry fired_markers 'period13_disc,period13,triple_gap,placeholder_payee' (and sometimes agency_benford) on one row.
fp_patterns.why_benign: All four markers are firing off one fact: a period-13 internal accrual has no assigned payee. That is the structural norm for these book entries (the guard text even says NULL payee must not itself raise severity), so the stacked score is double/triple-counting a single benign condition.

recommended_suppressions: For period13 and period13_disc: exclude internal book entries -- AND (payment_method IS NULL OR payment_method NOT IN ('INTERNAL','JOURNAL VOUCHER','COST ALLOCATION')). This removes 370/486 period13 and 181/192 period13_disc rows that are unverifiable journal accruals.

For period13 and period13_disc: require a readable counterparty -- AND upper(coalesce(payee,'NULL')) NOT IN ('N/A','NULL','NONE','VARIOUS') AND payee IS NOT NULL. (100% of period13_disc rows fail this and cannot be reviewed per the 'read the payee' discipline; keep only the few rows that name a real external vendor.)

For june_round (and apply consistently to period13 family): add the same arms-length suppression already used on round_1m/round_100k -- AND upper(coalesce(payee,'')) <> upper(agency) AND NOT regexp_matches(upper(payee),'\b'||regexp_escape(upper(agency))||'\b') AND NOT regexp_matches(upper(agency),'\b'||regexp_escape(upper(payee))||'\b'). This drops the ~$1.0B PSPRS self-pay and other agency=payee book transfers (19 rows).

For june_round: drop the INTERNAL/JV/COST-ALLOCATION rails (51 rows) and NULL/N-A payees (83 rows). Net effect: 191 june_round rows -> ~89 clean arms-length external leads (CENTURION/NAPHCARE/CORIZON prison healthcare, CGI/KYNDRYL IT, DOA risk-management law firms, GILA RIVER NRCD $7.5M) -- the genuine use-it-or-lose-it signal is preserved.

Optional de-stacking: when a period13/period13_disc row's null-payee already trips placeholder_payee and triple_gap, suppress the redundant markers (or cap combined severity) so a single benign null-payee book entry isn't counted 3-4x.

Belt-and-suspenders for whole family: exclude entitlement/scheduled categories explicitly on period13 -- AND category1 NOT IN ('AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS','DEBT SERVICE','TRANSFERS OUT','COST ALLOCATION AND INDIRECT COSTS','PERSONAL SERVICES'). period13_disc/june_round already whitelist discretionary categories, but the broad period13 marker does not and is dominated by AID ($1.64B), DEBT SERVICE ($571M, RBC Capital 1099-INT), and TRANSFERS OUT ($562M).
redteam.notes: Marker family is worth keeping but only via june_round after suppressions; period13/period13_disc as currently specified are near-pure structural noise. Definitions: period13=p13_adjustment_period_highvalue (amount>=100k AND fp_canon=13); period13_disc=p13_discretionary_closing_entry (adds discretionary category whitelist); june_round=june_useitorloseit_round_discretionary (amount>=100k AND fp_canon=12 AND discretionary cat AND round_100k/1m). fp_canon=13 is the post-close period-13 adjustment window (posts Jul-Aug); fp_canon=12 is June. Counts: period13=486 rows/$2.93B, period13_disc=192/$120M, june_round=191/$1.18B, union=677/$4.11B. Decisive evidence: period13_disc is 94% INTERNAL-rail and 100% null-payee (zero reviewable counterparties); period13 is 76% INTERNAL / 97% null-payee; june_round is 53% structurally benign (19 self-pay incl. $1.0B PSPRS + 51 internal + 83 null-payee) leaving ~89 clean arms-length leads. The june_round PSPRS $1B self-pay is a guard-acknowledged-but-unsuppressed miss. Separately, PITNEY BOWES tripping person_name_payee is a real FP but belongs to a different marker family, not yearend. Data: mart/tier_detail.parquet; PSPRS history cross-checked in mart/hv_base.parquet; predicate definitions in mart/used_predicates.json.

redteam.est_false_positive_share: ~50-55% of fires are benign-by-structure (roughly 1,200-1,250 of 2,278 peer_outlier rows). The remaining ~45% (named third-party vendors, real contracts) is genuine, review-worthy signal that must be preserved.

fp_patterns.pattern: MAD-collapse: extreme robust_z driven by a sparse/near-constant peer group, not by a large payment. When an agency x category1 cell has very few rows clustered at a near-identical value, MAD approaches 0 and the robust z explodes even for a modest dollar amount. The top z-scores in the whole marker are the SMALLEST dollars.
fp_patterns.example: SECRETARY OF STATE (DEPT OF STATE) / CAPITAL EQUIPMENT: a $236,950 payment to INTERIOR SOLUTIONS OF ARIZONA LLC scores robust_z=354,904 (vs the entire ADOT $30M+ contracts that score z~880). The peer cell has only 1-13 rows/year tightly clustered at ~$52K (FY18-20), so MAD is near-zero. amount<->z correlation across all fires is only 0.28; 5 of the 6 fires with z>=1000 are under $5M.
