fp_patterns.why_benign: A $139K-$236K capital-equipment purchase in a tiny agency is unremarkable in absolute terms. The astronomical z is a pure statistical artifact of an under-populated peer group, not evidence of concentration or discretion. These are noise, not leads.

fp_patterns.pattern: Internal interagency / cost-allocation transfers with no external payee. payment_method IN ('INTERNAL','COST ALLOCATION') and payee is 'N/A'/NULL. These are intra-government fund moves (operating-lump-sum clearing, joint funds, allocations), benign-by-structure per caveat #3, and not reviewable as a vendor lead.
fp_patterns.example: 692 fires (~30% of all fires, $3.18B) are unnamed-payee + INTERNAL/COST ALLOCATION. e.g. DEPT OF CORRECTIONS 'NON-HEALTH CARE AOOE'/'PRIVATE PRISON COP' $17.4M INTERNAL, z=444, payee N/A; DEPT OF EMERGENCY & MILITARY AFFAIRS Border Security Fence $91.97M INTERNAL transfer, z=1836, payee N/A.
fp_patterns.why_benign: No external counterparty exists; these are appropriation/clearing transfers between state funds and agencies. There is nothing for a bondholder to vet and the z is inflated by the same lumpy-transfer dynamic.

fp_patterns.pattern: Agency-pays-itself / university self-payee. Rows where payee == agency (or a university paying a university). These are operating-fund draws / internal disbursements booked as OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES, not third-party spend.
fp_patterns.example: NORTHERN ARIZONA UNIVERSITY -> NORTHERN ARIZONA UNIVERSITY, 145 fires, $1.40B, z up to 153; UNIVERSITY OF ARIZONA (FINANCIAL SERVICES OFFICE) -> itself, 25 fires, $936M; ARIZONA STATE UNIVERSITY -> itself, 14 fires, $1.04B. 195 self-pay rows totaling $3.78B.
fp_patterns.why_benign: A self-directed payee is an internal accounting posting, not a procurement. The university is the only large 'vendor' in its own agency-category, so it is always an outlier by construction.

fp_patterns.pattern: Benign-by-structure program payments per caveats: Lottery retailer commissions/transfers to banks, Medicaid/AHCCCS capitation & DDD long-term-care premium tax, debt service, pension, and federal aid/grant pass-throughs.
fp_patterns.example: LOTTERY COMMISSION -> BANK OF AMERICA-AZ $90-92M, z up to 490 (prize/sweep), and 'SALES COMMISSIONS TO TICKET RETAILERS' $422M; DES 'DDD PREMIUM TAX LTC' Medicaid LTC capitation, dozens of rows z=130-340 (description is a junk placeholder 'ONLY UPDATE THE DESCRIPTION FOR FY18...'); appropriations 'DEBT SERVICE' $273M, 'FEDERAL AID' $1.31B / 'FEDERAL GRANTS' $152M.
fp_patterns.why_benign: Explicitly named benign-by-structure flows in caveats #3/#4: managed-care capitation (AHCCCS->plans, DDD LTC), Lottery->bank sweeps, scheduled debt service, and federal pass-throughs. They are large by design and dominate their agency-category, so they always trip the peer z.

recommended_suppressions: Kill MAD-collapse: require BOTH a high z AND a meaningful peer group + absolute floor. Suppress when the agency x category1 peer cell is sparse or the amount is small despite huge z. Practical guardrail: do not fire peer_outlier when robust_z_in_agencycat >= 1000 (cap effective z, or require amount >= 5,000,000 for z>1000). Better: precompute per-cell peer_n and peer_mad and require peer_n >= 8 AND peer_mad > 0 before trusting the z; otherwise downgrade to a magnitude-only check. This removes the SoS Capital-Equipment z=354,904 artifacts (5 of 6 z>=1000 fires are <$5M) without touching ADOT/AshBritt (z 40-880 at $20M-$92M).

Exclude internal transfers with no external counterparty: AND NOT (payment_method IN ('INTERNAL','COST ALLOCATION') AND (payee IS NULL OR payee='N/A')). Removes ~692 fires / $3.18B that are intra-government clearing/allocation with nothing to review.
Exclude self-payee postings: AND NOT (upper(coalesce(payee,'')) = upper(agency)) AND NOT (payee LIKE '%UNIVERSITY%' AND agency LIKE '%UNIVERSITY%'). Removes ~195 self-directed rows / $3.78B.

Down-weight (annotate, don't necessarily drop) the named benign-by-structure flows already covered by caveats: agency='LOTTERY COMMISSION' with payee in major banks or appropriation LIKE '%SALES COMMISSIONS TO TICKET RETAILERS%'/'%PRIZE FUND%'; agency='AHCCCS' or appropriation_1_name LIKE '%PREMIUM TAX%' OR '%LONG TERM CARE%' OR '%CAPITATION%' (Medicaid managed care); appropriation_1_name LIKE '%DEBT SERVICE%'/'%PENSION%'/'%PSPRS%'/'%ASRS%'; appropriation_1_name LIKE 'FEDERAL AID%' OR 'FEDERAL GRANT%' (pass-throughs).

PRESERVE the genuine signal explicitly: keep fires where payee is a distinct external commercial vendor with vendor_share_of_agency_year material or a recognizable discretionary contract (e.g. CONNECT 202 PARTNERS, KIEWIT-FANN JV, COFFMAN AMES JV, ASHBRITT INC share=0.36, NAPHCARE, MOTOROLA, CGI, DELOITTE, LAKESHORE LEARNING z=377). Do NOT add a blanket 'university payee' exclusion or you will drop legitimate IGAs like ASU->DHHS research; only exclude university-pays-itself.
redteam.notes: Marker is worth keeping: after suppressing the four structural buckets, the surviving ~45% is high-quality bondholder signal -- large, concentrated, discretionary third-party contracts (freeway design-build JVs, the AshBritt border-fence emergency contractor, prison healthcare, DPS radios, IT consulting), which is exactly the 'lumpy capital / one big legit contract among small ones' set worth a manual read. Key structural insight to communicate to the model owners: robust_z is being treated as if monotone in dollar risk, but its top values are anti-correlated with dollar amount (corr ln(amt) vs ln(z) = 0.28). The single most damaging FP source is MAD-collapse in thin peer cells, which manufactures z-scores in the hundreds-of-thousands for sub-$250K payments -- a peer_n / peer_mad guardrail and an absolute-amount floor fix it cleanly. Two data caveats observed while auditing: transaction_description is 'N/A' or NULL for ~98% of fires (1,813 'N/A' + 418 NULL of 2,278), so description-based triage is impossible here; and the only populated descriptions are a junk placeholder ('ONLY UPDATE THE DESCRIPTION FOR FY18 AND...') on DES DDD long-term-care capitation -- do not mistake that text volume for a real anomaly. Source files: /Users/moeasnani/Documents/Openbooks/mart/tier_detail.parquet (fires) and /Users/moeasnani/Documents/Openbooks/mart/hv_base.parquet (used to confirm the sparse SoS peer cell).

redteam.est_false_positive_share: ~65-70% of all rail-family fires are benign-by-structure (11,738 of 16,931 covered by clean benign predicates). The nonstd_rail (WARRANT) variant is ~70-80% FP; manual_rail/manual_rail_disc (INTERNAL/JV) is ~99% non-vendor accounting entries.

fp_patterns.pattern: nonstd_rail fires on WARRANT, which is a normal external disbursement rail (State Treasurer paper check), not a manual/internal rail. WARRANT = 11,167 of 16,931 family fires (66%); it is paid to 7,379 distinct external vendors and is the same order of magnitude as ACH/PAYMENT in the full universe (103,506 rows / $18B). It is NOT a 'non-standard' rail at all.
fp_patterns.example: THE UNIVERSITY OF ARIZONA OPERATING LUMP SUM $85.9M; DEPT OF HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES FEDERAL PAYMENTS $14.5M; MARICOPA COUNTY FEDERAL AID $15.6M -- all paid by WARRANT, flagged nonstd_rail.
fp_patterns.why_benign: A warrant is the standard paper-check rail of the AZ Treasurer used for IGAs, federal pass-throughs and large one-off/land payments. Being paid by check is not a control weakness; the rail is fully external and auditable.

fp_patterns.pattern: 45% of nonstd_rail (WARRANT) fires carry a documented contract_name and/or contract number, i.e. they are scheduled payments on a named major contract -- exactly the 'documented major contract' benign class. 5,033 of 11,167 WARRANT fires have a real contract_name.
fp_patterns.example: CENTURION OF ARIZONA LLC contract 'INMATE CORRECTIONAL HEALTHCARE ADOC18-216360' $8.97M; CARDINAL HEALTH 'MMCAP PHARMACEUTICAL WHOLESALER'; CONNECT 202 PARTNERS contract CH882701C $12.8M; DELOITTE 'ATLAS REPLACEMENT' ERP $7.9M.
fp_patterns.why_benign: Documented inmate-healthcare, pharmaceutical, ADOT freeway and ERP contracts. The payment rail is incidental; the contract is named and on file.

fp_patterns.pattern: ADOT right-of-way / land condemnation paid to title & escrow agencies under FEDERAL AID appropriations. 357 fires are title/escrow agencies; these are by-law warrant disbursements for property acquisition.
fp_patterns.example: SECURITY TITLE AGENCY INC FEDERAL AID $28.3M; TITLE ALLIANCE OF THE VALLEY AGENCY LLC $4.9M; PIONEER TITLE AGENCY INC $3.4M -- ADOT freeway right-of-way.
fp_patterns.why_benign: Land/right-of-way acquisition for federal-aid highways is paid via title/escrow companies by warrant; round/whole-dollar amounts are negotiated settlement prices, not structuring.

fp_patterns.pattern: manual_rail / manual_rail_disc fire on INTERNAL and JOURNAL VOUCHER rows that have NO external payee -- they are internal cost-allocation, lump-sum clearing, and fund-transfer accounting entries, not disbursements. 5,757 of 5,764 INTERNAL/JV fires (99.9%) have payee N/A or NULL.
fp_patterns.example: payee 'N/A', appropriation 'DBME OPERATING LUMP SUM CLEARING' $10.7M (JOURNAL VOUCHER); 'DCS OPERATING LUMP SUM' $9.4M; description 'ONLY UPDATE THE DESCRIPTION FOR FY18' (a reclass entry).
fp_patterns.why_benign: These are inter-fund journal entries / cost allocations with no vendor. They are the legitimate inter-fund use the rail concept targets, but with a placeholder payee they cannot be vendor-level findings; they belong in a cost-allocation review track, not a vendor lead.

fp_patterns.pattern: Marker's payment-method list is mis-specified: COST ALLOCATION -- the single most unambiguously internal cost-allocation rail -- is NOT flagged at all (0 of 1,020 in-tier rows), while external WARRANT checks ARE. The marker whitelists a true inter-fund rail and flags a normal external rail.
fp_patterns.example: COST ALLOCATION: 1,020 tier rows, 0 rail-flagged. WARRANT: external checks to 7,379 vendors, 11,167 flagged.
fp_patterns.why_benign: Demonstrates the trigger list is backwards/incomplete and is the root cause of the WARRANT FP wave.

recommended_suppressions: Drop WARRANT from nonstd_rail entirely (it is a standard external disbursement rail). If a check rail must be watched, restrict to MANUAL/HANDWRITTEN warrant subtypes only -- a plain WARRANT should not fire: ... AND payment_method <> 'WARRANT'

For manual_rail / manual_rail_disc on INTERNAL/JOURNAL VOUCHER, exclude placeholder-payee accounting entries and route them to a separate cost-allocation review track instead of a vendor lead: AND NOT (payment_method IN ('INTERNAL','JOURNAL VOUCHER') AND (payee IS NULL OR upper(payee) IN ('N/A','NULL','')))

Suppress rows that carry a documented contract: AND NOT (contract_name IS NOT NULL AND upper(contract_name) NOT IN ('N/A','NULL',''))  -- removes ~5,033 WARRANT contract payments (Centurion, Cardinal Health, ADOT CH-series, Deloitte).

Suppress intergovernmental payees (IGAs/pass-throughs): AND NOT regexp_matches(upper(payee), 'UNIVERSITY|BOARD OF REGENTS| COUNTY|CITY OF|TOWN OF|HUMAN SERVICES|TREASURY|SCHOOL DISTRICT|COMMUNITY COLLEGE|WATER DISTRICT')

Suppress ADOT title/escrow right-of-way disbursements under federal-aid appropriations: AND NOT (upper(payee) LIKE '%TITLE %' OR upper(payee) LIKE '%ESCROW%' OR upper(appropriation_1_name) LIKE '%FEDERAL AID%')

Add the marker's own inverse sanity check: only fire on payment methods that are genuinely rare/manual for that agency-category (e.g., rail share < 5% of the agency-year), so a high-frequency rail like WARRANT cannot trip it.
redteam.notes: VERDICT: keep the manual_rail concept but split and re-scope it -- the family is currently mostly noise (~65-70% benign-by-structure). Root cause: the trigger payment-method list is mis-specified. nonstd_rail fires on WARRANT, which is the AZ State Treasurer's standard paper-check rail (103,506 rows / $18B in the full HV universe, paid to 7,379 distinct external vendors), NOT a manual/internal rail -- yet COST ALLOCATION, a genuinely internal rail, is whitelisted (0 fires). That is backwards. The top WARRANT 'hits' are textbook benign: University of Arizona IGA ($86M lump sum), HHS/Maricopa County federal pass-throughs, documented contracts (Centurion/NaphCare inmate healthcare w/ contract ADOC18-216360, Cardinal Health MMCAP pharma, Connect 202 / Pulice JV ADOT federal-aid construction CH-series, Deloitte ATLAS ERP), and ADOT title-agency right-of-way land acquisition. Separately, manual_rail/manual_rail_disc on INTERNAL/JOURNAL VOUCHER are 99.9% placeholder-payee internal accounting entries (lump-sum clearing, fund transfers, FY reclass 'ONLY UPDATE THE DESCRIPTION') -- legitimate inter-fund mechanics with no vendor, so they cannot support a vendor-level finding and should move to a cost-allocation track. GENUINE SIGNAL THAT SURVIVES: large INTERNAL/JV entries paired with OTHER strong markers (peer_outlier + placeholder_payee on $30-60M un-appropriated entries) are still worth a controls look, and any genuinely manual/handwritten warrant override -- so do not delete the marker, just stop it from firing on (a) plain WARRANT, (b) documented-contract rows, (c) intergov payees, and (d) placeholder-payee accounting entries. Applying the union of those four exclusions removes 11,738 of 16,931 fires while leaving the concentrated, undocumented, multi-marker leads intact. All evidence from mart/tier_detail.parquet and mart/hv_base.parquet under /Users/moeasnani/Documents/Openbooks.

redteam.est_false_positive_share: ~99% (8016/8016 are vid-NULL + payee N/A + zero contract + INTERNAL/JV; 99.9% have no real description; only 3 rows survive every benign filter, and even those are generic object-code text under $1M)

fp_patterns.pattern: Benign-by-structure flows routed on the manual/internal rail: Lottery retailer commissions, AHCCCS/DDD Medicaid premium-tax LTC, ASRS pension health-insurance subsidy, COP/debt service. All are INTERNAL or JOURNAL VOUCHER with no external payee by design.
fp_patterns.example: LOTTERY COMMISSION / SALES COMMISSIONS TO TICKET RETAILERS, INTERNAL, $26.5M, payee=NULL, desc=NULL; DEPT OF ECONOMIC SECURITY / DDD PREMIUM TAX LTC, INTERNAL, $20.5M
fp_patterns.why_benign: Caveat #4 explicitly: Lottery->banks, Medicaid managed-care, pension (ASRS), and debt service are benign-by-structure. They settle on the internal rail with no vendor, so the marker's three 'gaps' are inherent to the payment type, not evidence of opacity.

fp_patterns.pattern: Budget/appropriation-level INTERNAL allocations and ADOT journal-voucher transfers (federal-aid match, freeway/highway construction allocations, lump-sum appropriations, private-prison per-diem internal charges).
fp_patterns.example: DEPT OF EMERGENCY & MILITARY AFFAIRS / BORDER SECURITY FENCE, INTERNAL, $91.97M, payee=N/A, desc=N/A; ADOT / M-RARF CONSTRUCT FREEWAYS, JOURNAL VOUCHER, $35.4M
fp_patterns.why_benign: These are internal cost-allocation / budget-posting entries, not disbursements to a third party. There is no vid, payee, or contract because no external party is paid; the JV/INTERNAL rail is the correct convention for these moves.

fp_patterns.pattern: Marker fires on the ABSENCE of a description rather than the content of one — directly violating the team rule 'READ the description, never infer.' 99.9% of fires have description = 'N/A', NULL, or a chart-of-accounts housekeeping note.
fp_patterns.example: 5539 rows desc='n/a', 2381 rows desc=NULL; the only recurring non-blank text is 'ONLY UPDATE THE DESCRIPTION FOR FY18 AND GOING FORWARD. DID NOT CREATE A NEW OBJ.' (89 rows) and 'FY 2018 COA CHANGES'
fp_patterns.why_benign: There is nothing to read. The 'discretionary' leg cannot be verified from data because the descriptive field is structurally empty on the internal rail. A lead with no payee, no vid, no contract, and no description is not actionable for a bondholder/auditor.

fp_patterns.pattern: Redundant with placeholder_payee: triple_gap co-fires with placeholder_payee on 100% of rows (8016/8016) and adds no independent signal.
fp_patterns.example: All 8016 fired_markers arrays contain both 'triple_gap' and 'placeholder_payee'; top fires also carry peer_outlier
fp_patterns.why_benign: The 'null vid + manual rail' legs are fully captured by placeholder_payee. triple_gap is a duplicate dimension that only inflates risk_score and lead count without isolating any new entity.

recommended_suppressions: Retire or demote triple_gap to a non-scoring tag: it co-fires with placeholder_payee on 100% of rows and contributes no independent signal. At minimum, stop letting it add to risk_score / tier.

Require a readable description before firing (enforces the 'READ, never infer' rule): AND transaction_description IS NOT NULL AND lower(transaction_description) NOT IN ('n/a','') AND upper(transaction_description) NOT LIKE 'ONLY UPDATE THE DESCRIPTION%' AND upper(transaction_description) NOT LIKE '%COA CHANGE%' AND upper(transaction_description) NOT LIKE 'FY %COA%'. This alone removes 99.9% of current fires.

Exclude benign-by-structure flows on the internal rail: AND agency NOT IN ('LOTTERY COMMISSION','AHCCCS') AND agency NOT LIKE '%RETIREMENT SYSTEM%' AND appropriation_1_name NOT LIKE '%PREMIUM TAX%' AND appropriation_1_name NOT LIKE '%HEALTH INSURANCE%' AND appropriation_1_name NOT LIKE '%DEBT SERVICE%' AND appropriation_1_name NOT LIKE '%COP %' AND appropriation_1_name NOT LIKE 'FEDERAL AID%' AND appropriation_1_name NOT LIKE 'FEDERAL GRANT%'.

Suppress pure budget/cost-allocation postings that have no external counterparty: when payee IN ('N/A','') OR payee IS NULL combined with the empty-description condition, treat as internal accounting and do not flag (these have no vid, payee, contract, OR description — nothing an auditor can act on).

If kept at all, restrict to JOURNAL VOUCHER rows above a high dollar threshold that ALSO have a substantive (non-object-code, non-COA) description, then route to manual review rather than auto-tiering — expect a handful of rows, not 8016.
redteam.notes: Verdict: false_positive_marker (systematic misfire). triple_gap is defined as null-vid + manual rail + discretionary, but in the data 100% of its 8016 fires are payment_method INTERNAL (6729) or JOURNAL VOUCHER (1287) with payee = N/A/NULL and ZERO contract_name and ZERO contract_number populated, and 99.9% have no usable transaction_description (5539 'n/a', 2381 NULL, plus ~91 chart-of-accounts housekeeping notes). It therefore flags the internal-accounting rail itself, where the three 'gaps' are inherent to the payment type. The top fires are textbook benign-by-structure items from caveat #4 (Lottery retailer commissions $595M, DDD Medicaid premium-tax LTC $627M, ASRS pension health-insurance subsidy $293M, COP debt service $429M) plus internal budget allocations (border-security-fence $92M, ADOT M-RARF/federal-aid freeway JVs). Crucially the marker violates the team's hard rule: it fires on the ABSENCE of a description, so there is nothing to read and the 'discretionary' judgment cannot be verified from data. It is also fully redundant with placeholder_payee (co-fires 100%). Applying all proposed guardrails leaves only 3 rows, and even those are generic object-code text ('OTHER EXTERNAL COMPUTER PROCESSING, HOSTING...') under $1M with no payee/vid/contract — i.e., still not actionable. There is effectively no genuine signal to preserve. Recommendation: retire triple_gap (or demote to a non-scoring tag) and rely on placeholder_payee plus the magnitude/peer_outlier markers for these internal-rail rows.

redteam.est_false_positive_share: ~99% (14,878 of 15,047 fires are internal-accounting rows where no external payee can exist; the rest are aggregate convenience-codes. Effectively 0 rows are a genuine masked single external discretionary payment.)

fp_patterns.pattern: N/A or NULL payee on an INTERNAL / JOURNAL VOUCHER / COST ALLOCATION / NULL payment_method row. These are intra-government accounting entries (transfers, clearing, cost allocations, debt-service moves) that have NO external counterparty by construction, so 'N/A' is the mandated convention, not a masked payee. This is 14,878 of 15,047 fires (98.9%).
fp_patterns.example: DEPT OF ADMINISTRATION, appropriation 'COP DEBT SERVICE 2009 3RD SS CH 6 SEC 32', payment_method=INTERNAL, payee=NULL, $60,105,600 — Certificates-of-Participation debt service (benign-by-structure debt service).
fp_patterns.why_benign: Debt service / internal transfer booked via INTERNAL journal entry; there is no vendor, so payee is structurally N/A. Marker mistakes an accounting bookkeeping move for a masked discretionary payment.

fp_patterns.pattern: Aggregate / convenience payee codes used as a system convention for many small purchases booked as one line: 'PCARD VENDOR xxx' (purchasing-card aggregation), 'FUEL VENDOR xxx', 'MISCELLANEOUS VENDOR', 'MISCELLANEOUS CUSTOMER'. 166 fires. vid follows a fixed pattern (PCARDDTA0001, MISCCUSTOM, FUELPSA00001).
fp_patterns.example: DEPT OF PUBLIC SAFETY, 'DPS JOINT FUND', payment_method=ACH, payee='PCARD VENDOR PSA', vid=PCARDPSA0001, $294,902 — a rolled-up purchasing-card settlement.
fp_patterns.why_benign: These are the state's standard aggregation payee codes (one row stands for thousands of card swipes / fuel buys). The placeholder is intentional masking of MANY tiny vendors, not one masked discretionary external payee.

fp_patterns.pattern: Self-insurance / loss-fund claim payouts where the individual claimant is privacy-masked. Only 3 fires (payment_method=PAYMENT).
fp_patterns.example: DEPT OF ADMINISTRATION, 'WORKERS COMPENSATION LOSSES', payment_method=PAYMENT, payee='N/A', ~$105K — workers-comp claim disbursement.
fp_patterns.why_benign: Claimant PII is deliberately suppressed on loss-fund payouts; the appropriation explicitly names the program. Benign-by-structure entitlement-style payout.

fp_patterns.pattern: Chart-of-accounts maintenance / description-correction journal lines whose transaction_description is literally 'ONLY UPDATE THE DESCRIPTION FOR FY18 AND GOING FORWARD' or 'FY 2018 COA CHANGES'. These are bookkeeping reclass entries, not payments.
fp_patterns.example: DEPT OF ECONOMIC SECURITY, 'DDD PREMIUM TAX LTC', INTERNAL, desc='ONLY UPDATE THE DESCRIPTION FOR FY18 AND GOING FORWARD.', $20,452,019.
fp_patterns.why_benign: An internal reclass/relabel entry carries no external payee. The description self-documents it as a COA maintenance action.

recommended_suppressions: EXCLUDE internal-accounting methods, which can never have an external payee: AND (payment_method IS NULL OR payment_method IN ('INTERNAL','JOURNAL VOUCHER','COST ALLOCATION')) => DO NOT fire placeholder_payee. This alone removes ~14,878 of 15,047 fires (98.9%).

EXCLUDE the state's documented aggregate / convenience payee codes (they mask MANY vendors, not one): AND NOT (payee ILIKE 'PCARD %' OR payee ILIKE 'FUEL VENDOR%' OR payee ILIKE 'MISCELLANEOUS VENDOR' OR payee ILIKE 'MISCELLANEOUS CUSTOMER' OR vid IN ('MISCCUSTOM') OR regexp_matches(coalesce(vid,''),'^(PCARD|FUEL)')). Removes the 166 aggregate-code fires.

EXCLUDE benign-by-structure appropriations regardless of payee: AND NOT (appropriation_1_name ILIKE '%DEBT SERVICE%' OR appropriation_1_name ILIKE '%COP %' OR appropriation_1_name ILIKE '%CLEARING%' OR appropriation_1_name ILIKE '%CLR%' OR appropriation_1_name ILIKE '%FEDERAL AID%' OR appropriation_1_name ILIKE '%RARF%' OR appropriation_1_name ILIKE '%COMMISSIONS TO TICKET RETAILERS%' OR appropriation_1_name ILIKE '%WORKERS COMPENSATION LOSS%' OR appropriation_1_name ILIKE '%NON APPROPRIATED BENEFITS%').
EXCLUDE COA-maintenance / reclass lines: AND NOT (transaction_description ILIKE 'ONLY UPDATE THE DESCRIPTION%' OR transaction_description ILIKE '%COA CHANGE%').

REQUIRE a true external method before treating N/A as a 'masked' payee: only fire when payment_method IN ('ACH','WARRANT','PAYMENT') AND payee IS NULL/N/A AND payee NOT IN the aggregate-code list above. After the exclusions above this leaves essentially 0 rows in current data — i.e. the genuine signal the marker claims to catch does not exist here.

DO NOT trust robust_z_in_agencycat or vendor_share_of_agency_year on these rows: all N/A/NULL rows pool into one pseudo-vendor, inflating avg robust-z to 40-58 and share artificially. If the marker is kept, null out these stats when vid IS NULL so downstream tiers don't amplify the artifact.
redteam.notes: Verdict: false_positive_marker. The marker's premise — 'null/N-A payee on discretionary spend = a real masked external payment worth review' — is contradicted by the data in ~99% of fires. 14,878/15,047 (98.9%) sit on INTERNAL / JOURNAL VOUCHER / COST ALLOCATION / NULL payment methods, which are intra-government accounting entries with no external counterparty (debt service, clearing/lump-sum accounts, federal-aid pass-throughs, cost allocations, COA reclasses). Another 166 are documented aggregate convenience codes (PCARD/FUEL/MISCELLANEOUS VENDOR/CUSTOMER) that intentionally mask MANY tiny vendors as one line. Only 3 fires hit a true external method and those are privacy-masked workers-comp claim payouts (benign entitlement-style). Net: the marker is firing on the system's payee convention, not on hidden discretionary external payments. A telling artifact: spot-check shows placeholder payees appear on 174,223 PAYMENT-method rows in hv_base, yet only 3 of those fired here — the marker is implicitly selecting internal-accounting rows where N/A is GUARANTEED, which is why it misfires. Recommend disabling placeholder_payee as a standalone risk signal, or gating it to payment_method IN ('ACH','WARRANT','PAYMENT') AND payee NOT IN(aggregate codes) AND appropriation not in the benign list — after which it produces effectively no leads in this dataset. Source data: mart/tier_detail.parquet (fire population) and mart/hv_base.parquet (full-history context). No genuine review-worthy lead was found within this marker family.

redteam.est_false_positive_share: ~77% (14,456 of 18,685 offcontract fires are benign-by-structure; the >=$1M no_contract_named variant is ~88% ADOT CAPITAL OUTLAY construction)

fp_patterns.pattern: ADOT federal-aid / road construction draws (FEDERAL AID, M-RARF, HIGHWAY CONSTRUCTION, REHABILITATE PAVEMENT appropriations, and virtually all DEPT OF TRANSPORTATION CAPITAL OUTLAY). These run on engineering/construction contracts whose number is simply never linked into contract_number. This is the single largest block and is explicitly called out in the predicate's own guard as a known residual to de-prioritize.
fp_patterns.example: DEPT OF TRANSPORTATION -> CONNECT 202 PARTNERS / KIEWIT-FANN JOINT VENTURE / PULICE-FNF-FLATIRON JV / FNF CONSTRUCTION / GRANITE CONSTRUCTION, appropriation_1_name='FEDERAL AID' or 'M-RARF CONSTRUCT FREEWAYS', CAPITAL OUTLAY. no_contract_named: 1,296 of 1,474 fires are exactly DEPT OF TRANSPORTATION + CAPITAL OUTLAY ($4.2B).
fp_patterns.why_benign: Highway construction is competitively bid and runs on a construction contract; the contract_number field is just not populated for ADOT capital draws. ADOT populates contract_number on only 30% of eligible rows and 0% on CAPITAL OUTLAY. Absence carries no information here.

fp_patterns.pattern: ADOA self-insurance / employee-benefits / risk-pool / workers-comp payments. Appropriations NON APPROPRIATED BENEFITS PROGRAM, RISK MANAGEMENT LOSSES AND PREMIUMS, WORKERS COMPENSATION LOSSES PREMIUMS, HEALTH PLAN SERVICES, 911 EMERGENCY TELECOMMUNICATION — paid to the state's health/dental/workers-comp plan administrators.
fp_patterns.example: DEPT OF ADMINISTRATION -> BLUE CROSS BLUE SHIELD OF AZ ($2.0B), MEDIMPACT HEALTHCARE SYSTEMS ($1.6B), UNITED HEALTHCARE ($1.4B), AETNA, CIGNA (CONNECTICUT GENERAL LIFE), DELTA DENTAL, CORVEL (workers comp). 4,223 fires / $7.3B, appropriation = 'NON APPROPRIATED BENEFITS PROGRAM'.
fp_patterns.why_benign: Premium/capitation/claims-admin payments out of the self-funded benefits and risk pools — benign-by-structure (entitlement-like, scheduled, plan-administrator payees), not discretionary procurement.

fp_patterns.pattern: Lottery prize-fund disbursements to the depository bank.
fp_patterns.example: LOTTERY COMMISSION -> BANK OF AMERICA - AZ, appropriation 'PRIZE FUND'/'PRIZE FUND REVENUE', 1,470 fires / $1.24B.
fp_patterns.why_benign: Lottery -> bank is explicitly benign-by-structure; these are prize payouts through the depository, not a procured service.

fp_patterns.pattern: Intergovernmental payments (IGAs / pass-throughs) to counties, cities, universities, community-college districts, the US Treasury/HHS, USPS, FBI, and utility/irrigation districts.
fp_patterns.example: DEPT OF TRANSPORTATION -> MARICOPA COUNTY ($109M), SALT RIVER PROJECT; DEPT OF PUBLIC SAFETY -> FEDERAL BUREAU OF INVESTIGATION; STATEWIDE SYSTEMS PROCESSING -> DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES; payees containing UNIVERSITY/COUNTY/CITY OF.
fp_patterns.why_benign: Legitimate intergovernmental agreements / federal remittances; these are governed by IGAs, not vendor procurement contracts, so contract_number is expected to be empty.

fp_patterns.pattern: Postage and occupancy/lease clearing accounts. Appropriations ending in CLR (clearing) such as DES POST/SUPP CLR and ADMN OCCUPANCY CLR.
fp_patterns.example: DEPT OF ECONOMIC SECURITY -> PITNEY BOWES ($29M) and UNITED STATES POSTAL SERVICES (postage, appropriation 'DES POST/SUPP CLR'); -> CHANDLER ELLIOT PARTNERS LLC / PHOENIX VAN BUREN PARTNERS LLC (building rent, appropriation 'ADMN OCCUPANCY CLR').
fp_patterns.why_benign: Postage meters/USPS and recurring building-lease payments routed through internal clearing accounts are routine scheduled disbursements, not off-contract procurement. PITNEY BOWES is also the canonical structural-FP payee.

fp_patterns.pattern: Zero-coverage agencies: agencies that populate contract_number on 0.0% of ALL eligible high-value rows, so the marker fires on every transaction regardless of whether a contract exists.
fp_patterns.example: ARIZONA STATE RETIREMENT SYSTEM (354), SUPREME COURT (247), GOVERNOR'S OFFICE (193), EXPOSITION AND STATE FAIR BOARD (114), SECRETARY OF STATE (86); also UNIVERSITY OF ARIZONA / ASU / NAU / BOARD OF REGENTS / ARIZONA COMMERCE AUTHORITY all at 0.0% coverage. ~1,269 fires sit in agencies that never key the field.
fp_patterns.why_benign: When an agency keys contract_number for 0% of its eligible spend, absence of a contract number is a property of the agency's accounting feed, not of the transaction — it carries zero discriminating signal.

recommended_suppressions: Suppress (or hold at severity 1) all DEPT OF TRANSPORTATION CAPITAL OUTLAY and federal-aid/road appropriations: NOT (agency='DEPT OF TRANSPORTATION' AND (category1='CAPITAL OUTLAY' OR appropriation_1_name='FEDERAL AID' OR regexp_matches(upper(appropriation_1_name),'M-RARF|HIGHWAY CONSTRUCTION|REHABILITATE PAVEMENT|CONSTRUCT FREEWAY|PAVEMENT|FREEWAY|BRIDGE'))) -- this alone removes the bulk of fires and is the predicate's own named residual; no_contract_named becomes ~mute for ADOT.

Exclude ADOA self-insurance/benefit/risk-pool appropriations: AND NOT regexp_matches(upper(appropriation_1_name),'BENEFITS PROGRAM|RISK MANAGEMENT|WORKERS COMP|HEALTH PLAN SERVICES|911 EMERGENCY') -- removes BCBS/MedImpact/UnitedHealthcare/Aetna/Cigna/Delta/Corvel capitation & premium.
Exclude Lottery prize fund: AND NOT (agency='LOTTERY COMMISSION' OR regexp_matches(upper(appropriation_1_name),'PRIZE FUND')).
Exclude debt service / investment management / banking fee appropriations: AND NOT regexp_matches(upper(appropriation_1_name),'DEBT SERVICE|INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT|BANKING FEE').

Exclude clearing-account postage and occupancy/lease: AND NOT regexp_matches(upper(appropriation_1_name),'POST/SUPP|POSTAGE|OCCUPANCY|OCCP') AND payee NOT IN ('PITNEY BOWES','UNITED STATES POSTAL SERVICES').

Exclude intergovernmental payees: AND NOT regexp_matches(upper(payee),'( |^)COUNTY|CITY OF|TOWN OF|UNITED STATES|US TREASURY|FEDERAL BUREAU|SALT RIVER PROJECT|UNIVERSITY|BOARD OF REGENTS|HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES|COMMUNITY COLLEGE|INDIAN|TRIBE|TRIBAL' ) AND NOT (upper(payee) LIKE '%NATION%' AND category2 LIKE '%INTERGOVERNMENTAL%'); also exclude AHCCCS managed-care.

Add a structural dark-switch: do NOT fire when the (agency, fiscal_year) pair populates contract_number on 0% of its eligible high-value rows -- i.e. suppress for ASRS, SUPREME COURT, GOVERNOR'S OFFICE, SECRETARY OF STATE, EXPOSITION/STATE FAIR BOARD, all three universities and BOARD OF REGENTS, ARIZONA COMMERCE AUTHORITY. Compute per agency-year: HAVING count(contract_number)=0. This generalizes the existing vid-NULL year guard to a coverage guard so the marker only fires where contract_number is sometimes populated (so absence is meaningful).

Optional precision lift: require transaction_description to be non-blank OR risk_score above a floor, since 99.8% of offcontract fires have transaction_description = N/A/NULL and the field adds nothing; pairing offcontract with at least one independent corroborating marker would sharply raise precision.
redteam.notes: VERDICT: keep the marker but heavily re-weight; it is a real but very noisy screen, not an accusation. Coverage facts: contract_number is ~9% populated mart-wide but among offcontract-ELIGIBLE rows it is actually 67% populated for PROFESSIONAL SERVICES and 36% for OTHER OPERATING -- so absence is more meaningful than the headline 4-9% suggests, EXCEPT in specific agencies/categories where coverage is structurally 0% (all 3 universities, ASRS, Supreme Court, Governor's Office, Secretary of State, Commerce Authority, Exposition Board) and in ADOT CAPITAL OUTLAY (0% on capital). FP quantification: ~77.4% (14,456/18,685) of fires are benign-by-structure under a conservative dedup filter; bucketed ~36.6% ADOT fed-aid/construction, ~22.6% ADOA benefits/risk pools, ~7.9% Lottery prize, plus IGAs/Medicaid/postage/occupancy/pension/debt. The >=$1M 'no_contract_named' variant is essentially an ADOT-capital detector (88% of its fires) and should be suppressed there entirely. SIGNAL PRESERVED: the ~20% residual (3,749 rows, up to risk_score 27) concentrates in genuinely discretionary agencies (Corrections, DES, Health Services, DPS, Veterans, Water Resources) and surfaces real review-worthy IT/services procurements -- e.g. ELECTION SYSTEMS & SOFTWARE (voting equipment, Sec of State, HAVA), PCC TECHNOLOGY GROUP (database replacement), ASPEN MEDICAL (border-security services), GEO CORRECTIONS (private-prison per diem), MYTHICS/CDW/HP (IT). The proposed exclusions are all appropriation/payee/agency-coverage based, none of which touch this residual, so they suppress the structural FPs without killing the genuine signal. All findings are leads-to-confirm; absence of a contract number is never proof a contract does not exist. Key files: mart/tier_detail.parquet (fired rows), mart/hv_base.parquet (coverage context), mart/used_predicates.json (predicates 'disc_proc_offcontract_base' -> offcontract and 'large_procurement_no_contract_reference' -> no_contract_named).

redteam.est_false_positive_share: ~90% of rows (408/448) and ~75-80% of distinct payees. Of $161.9M flagged, ~$147M (91%) is to entities carrying an explicit corporate token. Genuine-individual residual: ~25 payees / 40 rows / $14.8M, and most of THAT is tort/workers-comp settlement payees (benign-by-structure), leaving only a tiny review-worthy core.

fp_patterns.pattern: Two-word ALL-CAPS company name with no Inc/LLC/Corp token slips the regex because it is structurally indistinguishable from FIRST LAST. Highest-volume offenders.
fp_patterns.example: PITNEY BOWES (170 rows, $56.1M, robust_z=41.5, appropriations = POSTAGE FUND / DES POST-SUPP CLR); RR DONNELLEY (41 rows, $7.4M, printing/OPERATING LUMP SUM); CALMAT CO; WW CLYDE; FISHEL CO; MUNSON BOATS; PARADIGM LABORATORIES; TUCSON WINNELSON; MP NEXLEVEL; UXO PRO (env remediation firm, $1.78M WQARF sites).
fp_patterns.why_benign: These are well-known commercial vendors (postage meters, commercial printing, ready-mix concrete, construction, lab services). The 'name' resembles a person only because two capitalized tokens collide with the FIRST-LAST shape; the appropriation/category (POSTAGE FUND, PRINTING, lab fees) is unambiguous routine procurement.

fp_patterns.pattern: Payee DOES carry a corporate/legal token (PC, APC, LP, LLC, LTD, CORP, CO, INC, USA, JV) but the regex still fires -- the suffix is not on its exclusion list. 79 rows / 11 payees / $26.0M.
fp_patterns.example: POLSINELLI PC, TRULAW PC, BUCHALTER APC (law firms); MARSH USA, WILLIS LIMITED (insurance brokers); SEGAL CO (actuary); FORNEY LP; TRIZETTO CROPORATION (note the typo 'CROPORATION'); PULICE-WADSWORTH JV (ADOT joint venture, $10.5M); ADELMAN GERMAN (law firm, $4.7M risk-mgmt defense).
fp_patterns.why_benign: Explicit entity suffix = company by definition. Law-firm / broker / actuary / JV. The marker should never treat a payee ending in a legal-entity token as a person name.

fp_patterns.pattern: Government / intergovernmental entity caught by the two-word-caps shape. 21 rows / 2 payees / $28.0M.
fp_patterns.example: STATE TREASURER (20 rows, $27.9M, COM COLLEGE REIMBURSEMENT ARS 15-1469 -- a statutory IGA pass-through, vendor_share_of_agency_year up to 0.24); US TREASURY (INDIRECT COSTS / federal remittance).
fp_patterns.why_benign: Intergovernmental transfer / statutory reimbursement to a government body, explicitly covered by caveat (3). Not a person and not discretionary.

fp_patterns.pattern: Genuine individual, but in RISK MANAGEMENT LOSSES / WORKERS COMPENSATION / FUND ADMINISTRATION appropriations = tort or workers-comp settlement payee. 25 rows / 11 payees / $13.5M (overlaps the personal residual).
fp_patterns.example: JANA HAPPEL, JOHN JARAB, DOLORES CAMACHO, RAINER GRUESSNER ($100K each), JAMES BUSTEAD (WORKERS COMP), TALONYA ADAMS ($600K RISK MGMT LOSSES).
fp_patterns.why_benign: Individuals are EXPECTED payees of legal settlements / WC claims; paying a person from a 'RISK MANAGEMENT LOSSES' or 'WORKERS COMPENSATION' line is benign-by-structure, not a sign of a discretionary contract to an insider.

fp_patterns.pattern: Individual who is actually a sole-proprietor contractor or right-of-way land seller on CAPITAL OUTLAY / FEDERAL AID / FREEWAY-construction lines.
fp_patterns.example: KENNETH PORTER (Dept of Corrections CAPITAL OUTLAY + 'ACI - OPERATIONS' = Arizona Correctional Industries, $1.09M); JOSE SORTO, PHIL TOOLE, VICTOR DAD, GONZALO MARQUEZ (ADOT M-RARF CONSTRUCT FREEWAYS / FEDERAL AID -- ROW parcel acquisition or construction).
fp_patterns.why_benign: Personal-looking names on freeway-construction / land-acquisition / correctional-industries appropriations are contractors or property sellers, explained by the capital-outlay appropriation context, not discretionary grants.

recommended_suppressions: Add a legal/entity-suffix exclusion: suppress person_name_payee when the payee matches a trailing entity token. SQL: AND NOT regexp_matches(payee, '(^| )(CO|CORP|CORPORATION|INC|LLC|L L C|LP|LLP|PC|APC|PLC|PLLC|LTD|LIMITED|USA|JV|GROUP|ASSOC|ASSOCIATES|PARTNERS|HOLDINGS|SOLUTIONS|SYSTEMS|SERVICES|ENTERPRISES|INDUSTRIES|LABORATORIES|LABS|CONSTRUCTION|CONTRACTING|BANK|TRUST)$'). This alone removes 79+ rows ($26M) of suffixed firms.

Maintain a known-commercial-vendor allowlist (no-suffix corporates) for the high-volume offenders: PITNEY BOWES, RR DONNELLEY, CALMAT, WW CLYDE, FISHEL, MUNSON BOATS, PARADIGM LABORATORIES, TUCSON WINNELSON, MP NEXLEVEL, UXO PRO, COLLEGE BOARD, MEASURED PROGRESS, WILLIAMS SCOTSMAN, FIRETRUCKS UNLIMITED. SQL: AND payee NOT IN (...). PITNEY BOWES + RR DONNELLEY alone are 211 rows / $63.5M (47% of all fires).

Exclude government/IGA payees: AND NOT regexp_matches(upper(payee), '(TREASURER|TREASURY|UNIVERSITY|COLLEGE|COUNTY|CITY OF|TOWN OF|STATE OF|DISTRICT|AUTHORITY|DEPARTMENT|DEPT|BOARD OF|HHS|IRS)'). Removes STATE TREASURER/US TREASURY (21 rows, $28M).

Downweight (do not hard-suppress) when appropriation_1_name ILIKE '%RISK MANAGEMENT%' OR '%WORKERS COMP%' OR '%LOSSES%' OR '%FUND ADMINISTRATION%' -- tort/WC settlement payees are expected individuals. Annotate as explained_benign rather than firing person_name.

Downweight person-name fires on CAPITAL OUTLAY / FEDERAL AID / freeway-construction (M-RARF) / right-of-way appropriations, where individual payees are typically ROW land sellers or sole-proprietor contractors (KENNETH PORTER, JOSE SORTO, PHIL TOOLE, VICTOR DAD).

Tighten the token rule itself: require BOTH tokens to look like name parts (alpha-only, not a known trade word like BOWES/CLYDE/CALMAT) AND require the row to lack any corporate suffix AND not match a vendor that also appears with corporate tokens elsewhere in hv_base. Consider firing only when the SAME individual receives multiple payments OR the appropriation is discretionary (grants/professional services) rather than settlements.
redteam.notes: Verdict: KEEP the marker but it is currently very low signal-to-noise -- ~90% of rows are structural false positives. Root cause: the 'two-word ALL-CAPS' heuristic has no corporate-suffix exclusion and no commercial-vendor allowlist, so (a) suffixed firms (PC/APC/LP/USA/JV/CO/CORP/INC) fire, (b) no-suffix two-word firms (PITNEY BOWES, RR DONNELLEY) fire, and (c) government bodies (STATE/US TREASURER) fire. The high robust_z values (41 for PITNEY BOWES, 34 for RR DONNELLEY, 28 for STATE TREASURER) are being driven by these FPs, inflating risk_score on benign rows. After applying the suffix-exclusion + commercial allowlist + gov-entity exclusion, the residual is ~25 individual payees / $14.8M; of those, the RISK-MANAGEMENT/WORKERS-COMP cluster (settlement payees) and CAPITAL-OUTLAY/FEDERAL-AID cluster (ROW/contractors) are explained_benign by appropriation context. The genuinely review-worthy core is a handful: e.g., individual payees on COVID/CARES lines (CANDICE CUOCO $660K CORONAVIRUS DISEASE 2019, JOANNE BOALER $167K CORONAVIRUS STATE & LOCAL FISCAL, KATHLEEN MALLOCH BNA PRIVATE GRANTS) and BROKER COMMISSIONS (JAMES ADAMS $283K) -- a bondholder might legitimately want to see large discretionary payments to named individuals outside settlement/capital lines. Per team discipline I did not infer wrongdoing; classification is by READ appropriation/category only. Marker family overall = false_positive_marker dominant, with a small mixed genuine core that only surfaces after the suppressions above. Caveat (1) NULL vendor_id 2019-2020 was not the driver here (PITNEY BOWES/RR DONNELLEY span all years); caveat (2) offcontract is irrelevant to this family.

redteam.est_false_positive_share: ~95-100% of flagged ROWS are benign-by-structure; effectively all 6 flagged AGENCIES fail for a measurement artifact, not anomalous spending.

fp_patterns.pattern: Agency-level blanket tag: agency_benford fires on ~100% of every Tier-1/2/3 row in the 6 flagged agencies regardless of the individual transaction. It is not a transaction-level signal at all; it inherits one agency-wide nonconformity statistic onto every row.
fp_patterns.example: DEPT OF PUBLIC SAFETY 6388/6388 rows tagged (100%); SCHOOLS FOR THE DEAF AND THE BLIND 754/754 (100%); CORPORATION COMMISSION 402/402 (100%); DEPT OF REVENUE 879/879 (100%).
fp_patterns.why_benign: A marker that fires on every row of an agency carries zero per-transaction discriminating power. It cannot point an auditor at WHICH payment to read; it only restates 'this agency's amount histogram doesn't match Benford,' which is an aggregate property, not a lead.

fp_patterns.pattern: Left-censoring at the $25K high-value floor mechanically destroys Benford's leading-1 mass. The mart is gated at amount>=$25K, which deletes the entire $10,000-$19,999 band (the single largest contributor to leading digit '1' under Benford). Result: a deterministic deficit of 1s and excess of 2s/3s in EVERY agency.
fp_patterns.example: DPS leading-digit: 1=14.6% (Benford 30.1%), 3=21.3% (Benford 12.5%). Schools D&B: 1=11.8%, 3=28.7%. Proof of censoring: count of $10K-$20K rows below the floor = 0 (all removed). The SAME signature appears system-wide across all 87 agencies: 15.5% leading-1, 21.7% leading-3 — the whole checkbook 'fails' Benford for this reason.
fp_patterns.why_benign: Benford's law only holds for un-truncated, scale-spanning data. A dataset hard-filtered at $25K cannot conform by construction. The nonconformity is a property of the filter, not of the agency's spending. Flagging it is testing an assumption the data is guaranteed to violate.

fp_patterns.pattern: Payroll / personal-services dominance. Benford's law does not apply to bounded, banded quantities like salaries and employee-related expenditures, which cluster in narrow pay-grade ranges. The flagged agencies are payroll-heavy, and the nonconformity tracks payroll.
fp_patterns.example: Payroll share of flagged rows: SCHOOLS D&B 78.8%, CORP COMMISSION 78.4%, ATTORNEY GENERAL 75.4%, DPS 70.6%. 52.8% of all benford-flagged rows are PERSONAL SERVICES with payee N/A. In DPS, the 'payee' is N/A (4038 rows) or NULL (1107 rows) — i.e. aggregate payroll lines with no vendor at all.
fp_patterns.why_benign: Payroll amounts are administratively set (salary tables, biweekly accruals), not free-running magnitudes, so their leading digits never follow Benford. An agency whose spend is majority payroll will always 'fail' Benford regardless of any irregularity.

fp_patterns.pattern: No genuine round-number-program concentration to back the signal. The standard alternative explanation for Benford failure — a few large round-dollar programs skewing digits — is absent here.
fp_patterns.example: round_100k fires on just 0.1% (DPS), 0.0% (Vets, Schools, Corp Comm) and only 3.5% (Revenue) of flagged rows. round-to-$100k share of amounts is 0.0-4.8%. The 1-deficit/3-excess is diffuse and identical to the system-wide population, i.e. it is the $25K-floor artifact, not a concentrated program.
fp_patterns.why_benign: There is no concentrated benign program AND no concentrated suspicious program; the digit distortion is fully accounted for by the censoring floor and payroll banding, which are structural, not behavioral.

recommended_suppressions: RETIRE agency_benford as a transaction-level/row-level marker. It tags ~100% of an agency's rows, so it can never localize a lead and adds only noise to risk_score. At minimum, never let it contribute to per-row risk_score or tier assignment.

If a Benford-style check is kept at all, run it ONLY on the uncensored full ledger (before the $25K high-value cut), since the >=25K floor deterministically deletes the leading-1 band and guarantees failure. Concretely: do not compute Benford on read_parquet('mart/hv_base.parquet')/tier_detail (both are >=$25K).

EXCLUDE payroll/banded categories from any Benford population: WHERE category1 NOT IN ('PERSONAL SERVICES','EMPLOYEE RELATED EXPENDITURES'). Salaries are administratively banded and cannot follow Benford.

EXCLUDE placeholder/non-vendor lines before the test: WHERE payee NOT IN ('N/A') AND payee IS NOT NULL AND upper(payee) NOT LIKE 'PCARD%' AND upper(payee) NOT LIKE '%PSA'. ~78% of flagged rows have no real payee; they are aggregate postings, not discrete payments.

Recompute conformity against a CENSORING-AWARE expected distribution (truncated/conditional Benford given amount>=$25K), not the textbook 30.1/17.6/... table. The current marker compares censored data to the uncensored Benford table, which is apples-to-oranges and fails 100% of the time.

Add a discriminator so the marker only survives if the digit anomaly is CONCENTRATED, not diffuse: require that removing the top round-number program/vendor materially restores conformity (e.g., the agency's MAD/chi-square drops below threshold once round_100k rows or the single largest vendor are excluded). The 6 current agencies would all drop out because their distortion is diffuse and matches the system-wide baseline (15.5% leading-1 across all 87 agencies).
redteam.notes: Verdict: false_positive_marker (systematic, not occasional). agency_benford is an aggregate, agency-level statistic stamped onto every Tier row of 6 of 87 agencies. The 'nonconformity' it detects is a measurement artifact with two structural causes, both proven from the data: (1) the >=$25K high-value floor left-censors the dataset and deletes the entire $10K-$19.9K band (verified: zero sub-floor rows survive), mechanically producing a leading-1 deficit (DPS 14.6% vs 30.1% expected) and 2/3 excess (DPS 3s = 21.3% vs 12.5%) — and the IDENTICAL signature appears across the whole checkbook (system-wide leading-1 = 15.5%), so the flagged agencies are not distinguishable from the population; (2) payroll/personal-services dominance (70-79% of flagged rows; 53% are PERSONAL SERVICES with payee N/A), and salary bands never follow Benford. The benign 'few round programs' explanation is ruled out: round_100k fires on 0-4.8% of rows. Net: the marker cannot localize any lead (fires on 100% of agency rows), tests a property the data is guaranteed to violate, and its 6 picks are artifacts of the $25K cut plus payroll. Suppress it from risk scoring; if retained, run only on the uncensored, non-payroll, real-vendor ledger against a censoring-aware expected distribution, and require the distortion to be concentrated (restored by removing the top program/vendor) rather than diffuse. Key files: /Users/moeasnani/Documents/Openbooks/mart/tier_detail.parquet (flagged rows) and /Users/moeasnani/Documents/Openbooks/mart/hv_base.parquet (the >=$25K censored universe that drives the artifact).

model.verification_summary:
Verified 60 vendor leads, 15 accountability-gap (agency) leads, and 12 marker-family red-teams against mart/tier_detail.parquet and mart/hv_base.parquet. Spot-checks confirmed every load-bearing claim: AshBritt $194.7M on contract CTR060412 (share 0.358), Fondomonte $7M round sole-source WARRANT from SLFRF (share 0.24), Active Resource Management $12.5M single ACH (z=133.7, share 0.354); placeholder_payee fires 99% on internal accounting rails (14,878/15,047 INTERNAL/NULL/JV/COST-ALLOCATION); the new_vendor family is 3,893/5,548 FY2021 fires (the vid-renumbering/NULL-blackout artifact, FY2019-2020 absent); triple_gap co-fires with placeholder_payee on 8,016/8,016 rows (100% redundant); person_name_payee is led by PITNEY BOWES (170), RR DONNELLEY (41), CALMAT, STATE TREASURER — all corporate/IGA misfires.

Headline distribution: of 60 vendor leads, ~45 resolve to explained_benign or false_positive_marker (self-insured employee health benefits at ADOA — BCBS/UnitedHealthcare; AHCCCS Medicaid managed care; PSPRS/ASRS pension paydowns; Lottery->Bank of America prize fund; ADOT highway-construction JVs and right-of-way title/escrow agencies; university operating lump-sum appropriations; HHS/county/university IGAs), 8 are mixed (single documented major contracts with a real overlay — NaphCare, Deloitte, GBC/Sundt/CRE-WPL ROW), and 7 are genuine_review with real public salience (AshBritt border barrier, Fondomonte, Active Resource Management water credits, Arizona Jewish Historical Society Holocaust-center earmark, Kinella Construction at the Legislature, Weil Gotshal outside counsel, IRCS microbusiness grant). All 15 agency "accountability gap" clusters resolve to a single systemic data-transparency convention — null/N-A payee on INTERNAL/JOURNAL-VOUCHER/COST-ALLOCATION rails for inter-fund moves, debt service, benefits-trust legs, and Medicaid/pension accounting — i.e. marker misfire, not missing accountability (one exception, Supreme Court, is mixed on an embedded peer_outlier large-lump-sum subset). Three markers (triple_gap, placeholder_payee, agency_benford) are recommended for retirement or demotion to non-scoring; the rest are KEEP-with-guardrails. Throughout, these remain leads-to-confirm, not findings of wrongdoing.

reclassifications.reason: FY2021 first_year_seen is the NULL-vendor_id blackout artifact; hv_base shows continuous ADOA self-insured employee-benefits payments back to FY2016 ($171M->$455M) under the same agency/appropriation. Not new, not discretionary.

reclassifications.entity: THE UNIVERSITY OF ARIZONA (PZ000004410) / ARIZONA STATE UNIVERSITY (11368201, IV0000000997) / NORTHERN ARIZONA UNIVERSITY (PZ000001572)
reclassifications.reason: Agency IS the university; rows are the State's OPERATING LUMP SUM appropriation disbursed to its own university (and CARES/ESSER pass-throughs for ASU). vendor_share_of_agency_year=1.0 and high z are structural self-payment artifacts, not procurement.

reclassifications.entity: MOTOROLA SOLUTIONS INC (IV0000016996) / GRANITE CONSTRUCTION CO (IV0000000009) / FNF CONSTRUCTION (IV0000002317) / MARICOPA COUNTY (PZ000010983) / DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES (VC0000006143)
reclassifications.reason: All show pre-2021 hv_base history (vid-blackout resurfacing) and/or are on named statewide contracts (Motorola CTR046830) or are intergovernmental (county aid, federal HHS remittance). offcontract is non-probative at ~2-6% contract population.

person_name regex misfire: PITNEY BOWES is a postage-meter company (category2 POSTAGE AND DELIVERY); STATE TREASURER is a government office (statutory community-college reimbursement). Neither is a person; round amounts are meter prefunding / formula distributions.

reclassifications.entity: 15 agency accountability-gap clusters (DES, DOT, ADOA, Lottery, Corrections, Child Safety, Health Services, ASRS, AHCCCS, DPS, DEMA, Early Childhood, Revenue, Education)
reclassifications.reason: ~99% are null/N-A payee on INTERNAL/JOURNAL-VOUCHER/COST-ALLOCATION rails — inter-fund moves, debt service, benefits-trust legs, Medicaid/pension accounting that have no external payee by design. Markers re-detect the internal-accounting rail, not missing accountability.

reclassifications.to: mixed — downweight ~600 internal-rail rows; KEEP the peer_outlier subset
reclassifications.reason: Bulk is the same internal-rail null-payee convention, but ~15 items (robust_z 20-40, ~$30M of OPERATING LUMP SUM / JUDICIAL GRANTS / case-management) are review-worthy on MAGNITUDE regardless of blank payee.

Both are single documented major contracts (NaphCare CTR060508 inmate healthcare; Deloitte CTR052815 ATLAS) so most markers downweight, but each carries a genuine residual: NaphCare's Feb-2026 federal receivership over AZ prison healthcare; Deloitte's dup_sameday_ext same-day exact-duplicate warrant pairs worth a double-payment eyeball.

marker_fixes.fix: Add a payee-name resurfacing guard: do NOT fire when upper(trim(payee)) already received >=$25K in any prior fiscal year in hv_base (NOT EXISTS prior-year payee match). Treat FY2021 as a vid-reset boundary and recompute vendor_first_year/years_active on a stable key (payee + vendor_id_code), not the re-minted vid. Confirmed: FY2021 = 3,893 of 5,548 fires (70%); ~80% of FY2021 'new' vendors had prior-year history (MedImpact, BCBS, ASU $3.99B, CoreCivic, Centurion, 5 ADOT JVs). This is the single highest-severity structural false positive in the model.

RETIRE as a standalone risk signal or gate strictly: only fire when payment_method IN ('ACH','WARRANT','PAYMENT') AND payee is genuinely blank AND payee NOT IN the aggregate convenience codes (PCARD%/FUEL VENDOR%/MISCELLANEOUS VENDOR/MISCELLANEOUS CUSTOMER). Verified 14,878/15,047 fires (99%) are on internal rails (INTERNAL/NULL/JV/COST-ALLOCATION) where a null payee is the mandated convention; after gating, effectively zero genuine masked-external-payment leads remain.

RETIRE or demote to a non-scoring tag: it co-fires with placeholder_payee on 8,016/8,016 rows (100% redundant), fires only on INTERNAL/JOURNAL-VOUCHER rows with zero contract and no readable description (99.9% N/A/NULL), and violates the team's READ-don't-infer rule by firing on the ABSENCE of a description. Top fires are benign-by-structure (Lottery commissions, DDD Medicaid premium-tax LTC, ASRS pension subsidy, COP debt service). No independent signal.

RETIRE as a transaction/row-level marker (never contribute to per-row risk_score): it tags ~100% of every row in 6 of 87 agencies, so it cannot localize a lead. The 'nonconformity' is a measurement artifact of the >=$25K high-value floor (left-censoring deletes the entire $10K-$20K leading-1 band; system-wide leading-1 = 15.5% vs Benford 30.1%) plus payroll/personal-services banding (70-79% of flagged rows). If kept, run only on the uncensored, non-payroll, real-vendor ledger against a censoring-aware expected distribution and require concentrated (not diffuse) distortion.

Add (1) a trailing legal/entity-suffix exclusion (CO|CORP|INC|LLC|LP|LLP|PC|APC|PLC|PLLC|LTD|USA|JV|GROUP|PARTNERS|LABORATORIES|CONSTRUCTION|BANK|TRUST...); (2) a known no-suffix commercial allowlist (PITNEY BOWES, RR DONNELLEY, CALMAT, WW CLYDE, PARADIGM LABORATORIES...); (3) a government/IGA exclusion (TREASURER|TREASURY|UNIVERSITY|COUNTY|CITY OF|BOARD OF). Verified ~90% of rows are FP (PITNEY BOWES 170 + RR DONNELLEY 41 + STATE TREASURER 20 lead the list). Feed the postage allowlist into round-family suppression too (PITNEY BOWES double-misfires).

Drop plain WARRANT from nonstd_rail entirely — it is the AZ Treasurer's standard external paper-check rail (103,506 rows / $18B, 7,379 distinct vendors), not a manual/internal rail; restrict to manual/handwritten warrant overrides. Route manual_rail/manual_rail_disc INTERNAL/JV placeholder-payee rows to a cost-allocation track, not a vendor lead. Also suppress when a documented contract_name exists or payee is intergovernmental. The trigger list is currently backwards (flags external WARRANT, whitelists internal COST ALLOCATION).

Add a structural dark-switch: do NOT fire for any (agency, fiscal_year) that populates contract_number on 0% of its eligible high-value rows (all 3 universities, ASRS, Supreme Court, Governor's Office, Secretary of State, Commerce Authority) and for ADOT CAPITAL OUTLAY (0% on capital). Suppress the named benign blocks (ADOT federal-aid construction, ADOA benefits/risk pools, Lottery prize fund, IGAs). ~77% of fires are benign-by-structure; the >=$1M no_contract_named variant is 88% ADOT capital. Never treat as corroboration at ~2-6% population.

Suppress on POSTAGE replenishment (payee/appropriation LIKE postage), pension UNFUNDED-LIABILITY paydowns (PSPRS/ASRS), Lottery->bank prize fund, ADOA RISK MANAGEMENT/insurance settlements, and ADOT right-of-way title agencies. Stop using whole_dollar as a standalone/score-boosting marker (13.2% universal base rate) — require co-fire with >=1 independent strong marker. Add the agency=payee arms-length guard to june_round (currently misses the ~$1B PSPRS self-pay). Guardrails clear ~21% of fires ($2.6B) with no genuine loss.

Require count(DISTINCT transaction_reference_id) > 1 within a dup group (only flag identical amounts paid under genuinely DIFFERENT GAX/PRC documents) — kills 97.8% of sameday and 93% of diffday groups, which are single-invoice accounting-line splits. Exclude AHCCCS from diffday (92% of rows = Medicaid capitation posting-window splits) and pension/trustee/IGA payees. Preserves ~67 genuine same-vendor exact-amount separate-document leads (PCG, Microsoft, Deloitte, NaphCare).

Fix MAD-collapse: require peer_n >= 8 AND peer_mad > 0 and an absolute-amount floor before trusting robust_z (top z-scores are anti-correlated with dollars — a $236K Secretary-of-State capital-equipment buy scores z=354,904). Exclude internal-transfer/no-external-payee rows and self-payee (payee=agency, university-pays-itself) rows. Down-weight named benign-by-structure flows (Lottery->bank, Medicaid capitation, debt service, federal aid). Preserves the ~45% genuine concentrated-third-party-contract signal.

Suppress the self-insured employee-benefits program (appropriation LIKE '%BENEFITS PROGRAM%' — UnitedHealthcare @ ADOA, $1.1B, biggest single dollar FP); add Lottery/treasury bank prize-fund to the vendor_dependency bank guard (reuse marker-1 word-boundary regex; 414 BofA rows); broaden the IGA/self-payment guard so payee-name need not equal agency-name (catches universities paid by non-university agencies, state-agency-to-state-agency, federal HHS). Preserves the genuine core (NaphCare, AshBritt, Off Madison Ave tourism PR, redistricting law firms).

marquee_interesting_entities.why_interesting: This vendor's only transaction in the entire dataset — a perfectly round, sole-source, off-contract $12.5M purchase from a private LLC where nearly every other large recipient of the fund is a government/tribal/municipal entity. Discretionary, concentrated, and publicly questioned as a high-priced private sale.
marquee_interesting_entities.public_context: ARM (owned by Vidler Water Company) sold 50,000 Long Term Storage Credits to the Arizona Water Banking Authority for $12.5M (~$250/credit); local reporting characterized it as a private sale at a notably high price relative to ARM's underlying acquisition cost.

marquee_interesting_entities.name: ARIZONA JEWISH HISTORICAL SOCIETY
marquee_interesting_entities.exposure: $7,000,000 — single FY2025 WARRANT, GENERAL FUND via DEPT OF EDUCATION, on named appropriation/contract 'ARIZONA HOLOCAUST EDUCATION CENTER' (CTR073049); robust_z 54.3
marquee_interesting_entities.why_interesting: A one-off, highly concentrated legislative earmark to a named private nonprofit — the vendor IS the entire appropriation line. Purpose is documented and benign, but it is a discretionary capital grant a bondholder would legitimately want surfaced.
marquee_interesting_entities.public_context: The Society received a $7M FY2025 state appropriation (sponsored by Reps. Alma Hernandez and David Marshall, distributed by Supt. Tom Horne) to help build the Hilton Family Holocaust Education Center in Phoenix, which broke ground in 2025 and is slated to open 2027; a condition was free admission for Arizona students.

marquee_interesting_entities.name: KINELLA CONSTRUCTION LLC
marquee_interesting_entities.exposure: $5.77M across 11 FY2023-24 rows (one $1.5M Tier-1), GENERAL FUND OPERATING LUMP SUM at the SENATE and HOUSE; 20.3% of Senate FY2023 spend; robust_z 29.1 (~24x category median)
marquee_interesting_entities.why_interesting: Discretionary, highly concentrated, exact-round, sole-source-style construction spend at the Legislature with no contract named and no transaction description. The $1.5M payment is ~24x the Senate R&M category median; Kinella alone took $3.9M of that category vs $118K for the next vendor. A legitimately review-worthy item, not a misfire.
marquee_interesting_entities.public_context: Kinella Construction LLC is a Phoenix Valley general contractor (AZ registry L11030783) doing commercial/multifamily/custom construction and remodels — a plausible counterparty for Arizona Senate/House building repair and capital-improvement work.

marquee_interesting_entities.name: WEIL GOTSHAL & MANGES LLP
marquee_interesting_entities.exposure: $1.5M across two FY2018 WARRANTs ($1M Tier-1 + $500K Tier-2) from ADOA RISK MANAGEMENT LOSSES AND PREMIUMS; vendor's only activity in the data
marquee_interesting_entities.why_interesting: A large, round, one-time, off-contract June disbursement from the state self-insurance fund to top-tier outside litigation counsel — discretionary, concentrated, and unusual. No specific public record of the engagement surfaced, so a bondholder would legitimately want it explained.
marquee_interesting_entities.public_context: Weil, Gotshal & Manges LLP is a top-tier international (AmLaw-100) law firm known for high-stakes M&A, restructuring, and complex/class-action litigation; retained as elite outside counsel by Arizona's Dept of Administration in 2018 with no specific public record of the matter.

marquee_interesting_entities.name: ADOT highway design-build joint ventures (Connect 202 Partners, Kiewit-Fann JV, Pulice-FNF-Flatiron JV, Coffman Ames JV, Sundt CS A JV)
marquee_interesting_entities.exposure: Hundreds of millions each — e.g. Connect 202 ~$1.66B Loop 202; Kiewit-Fann I-17 (~$315M Tier-1); Pulice-FNF-Flatiron ~$523M FY2021-25; all CAPITAL OUTLAY at ADOT
marquee_interesting_entities.why_interesting: Not flagged for concern (vendor_share is low single-digit %; markers are offcontract/new-vendor structural artifacts), but these are the State's largest discretionary public-works capital commitments and the recognizable face of ADOT megaproject spend — useful high-level context for a bondholder even though each is routine, documented, federally/RARF-funded construction.
marquee_interesting_entities.public_context: These JVs deliver Arizona's largest freeway projects under ADOT design-build/P3 contracts — the Loop 202 South Mountain Freeway (Connect 202, ~$1.66B, the state's first freeway P3), the I-17 Anthem-to-Sunset Point widening (Kiewit-Fann), and the Loop 101/Loop 202 corridors (Coffman Ames, Pulice-FNF-Flatiron).

marquee_interesting_entities.name: Redacted / individual / one-shot settlement payees (NAME REDACTED, MISCELLANEOUS CUSTOMER, plaintiff law firms in ADOA RISK MANAGEMENT)
marquee_interesting_entities.exposure: Many sub-$2M warrants from ADOA RISK MANAGEMENT LOSSES AND PREMIUMS and the AG Consumer Restitution fund (e.g. TruLaw PC $1.9M, NAME REDACTED $1.69M, MISCELLANEOUS CUSTOMER $15.1M aggregate)
marquee_interesting_entities.why_interesting: Structurally benign (legal settlements, restitution, statutory-cap restitution payouts, masked individual claimants) but a recurring transparency texture: large dollars flow to redacted/individual payees the checkbook does not name. Worth a single agency-level disclosure note for a bondholder rather than per-item review.
marquee_interesting_entities.public_context: Arizona's self-insurance / Risk Management fund pays tort and workers-comp settlements (overwhelmingly to plaintiff law firms and named claimants); the AG's Consumer Restitution fund disburses settlement proceeds to counties and class-action administrators; 'NAME REDACTED' and 'MISCELLANEOUS CUSTOMER' are payee-masking placeholders, not real entities.

overtaker_headline_findings: The model's biggest dollar clusters are benign-by-structure, not red flags: the State's self-insured employee health-benefits program (BCBS-AZ, UnitedHealthcare at ADOA), AHCCCS Medicaid managed-care capitation, PSPRS/ASRS pension paydowns, Lottery prize-fund sweeps to Bank of America, ADOT highway-construction JVs, and university operating-lump-sum appropriations together account for most flagged value and resolve to routine entitlement, intergovernmental, pension, debt-service, or documented-contract flows.

A single FY2021 data artifact drives ~70% of the new-vendor leads: vendor_id is NULL system-wide in FY2019-2020 and the vid numbering scheme was re-minted in FY2021, so long-standing payees (MedImpact, BCBS, ASU, CoreCivic, Centurion, five major ADOT contractors) are mislabeled 'new.' A payee-name resurfacing guard removes ~80% of FY2021 new-vendor fires while preserving the genuinely new ones.

All 15 agency 'accountability-gap' clusters (~$5B+) are one systemic accounting convention, not missing accountability: ~99% are null/N-A payee on INTERNAL/JOURNAL-VOUCHER/COST-ALLOCATION rails — inter-fund transfers, debt service, benefits-trust legs, and Medicaid/pension postings that have no external payee by design. The triple_gap and placeholder_payee markers re-detect the internal-accounting rail and should be retired or gated to external rails only.

After guardrails, a small high-quality shortlist of genuine leads survives — discretionary, concentrated, often sole-source spend to named private parties: Fondomonte ($7M federal-recovery payment to a Saudi-owned ag lessee), AshBritt ($194.7M border-barrier emergency contract), Active Resource Management ($12.5M water-credit purchase), an Arizona Holocaust-center earmark ($7M), Kinella Construction at the Legislature, and Weil Gotshal outside counsel. These are the items a bondholder should actually see.

Prison-healthcare concentration carries a live external overlay: NaphCare's ~$1.4B statewide inmate-healthcare contract (and its predecessor Centurion) is structurally a single documented contract, but a Feb-2026 federal-court receivership over Arizona prison healthcare with power to renegotiate or terminate the contract makes it review-worthy beyond the data alone.

Several markers fire on structure rather than behavior and need fixes: person_name_payee misfires on corporate names (PITNEY BOWES, RR DONNELLEY) and government offices (STATE TREASURER); agency_benford fails 100% of an agency's rows purely from the $25K high-value floor and payroll banding; offcontract is near-worthless where contract_number is 0% populated (all universities, ASRS, courts, ADOT capital); and nonstd_rail wrongly flags plain WARRANT, the Treasurer's standard external check rail.

The duplicate-payment markers are ~95-97% line-split artifacts but retain a real residual: most 'duplicates' are one invoice decomposed into accounting lines sharing a single GAX/PRC document, but requiring genuinely distinct transaction_reference_ids isolates ~67 legitimate same-vendor, identical-amount, separate-document leads worth a double-payment look (e.g. Deloitte same-day warrant pairs).

A persistent transparency texture, not wrongdoing: large dollars routinely flow to redacted, masked, or individual payees (NAME REDACTED, MISCELLANEOUS CUSTOMER, settlement-fund law firms and claimants) that the published checkbook does not name. This is best handled as a single agency-level disclosure-completeness note rather than thousands of per-item leads.
